Code for longitudinal SBP and SHT analysis in cats

C Pye

2026-06-18

#This R markdown file contains code used to examine changes in SBP and hypertension risk in 209 cats enrolled on CatPAWS. final edit to accompany manuscript

#1. Load libraries

library(lme4)
## Loading required package: Matrix
library("ggplot2")
library(splines)
library(dplyr)
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
##

library(sjPlot)

## The following object is masked from 'package:ggplot2':
## 
##     set_theme
library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tibble    3.3.0
## ✔ purrr     1.2.0     ✔ tidyr     1.3.1

library(mclogit)
library(performance)
library(gtsummary)
library(ggfortify)
library(ggridges)
library(patchwork)
library(see)
library(geepack)
library("survival")
library("ggsurvfit")
library("gee")
library("car")

#2. load data

Metadata_209_cats<-read.csv("Metadata_hypertension.csv")

#3. order factors in df

#4. create dataframe removing SBP NA

Metadata_SBP<-Metadata_209_cats%>%
  filter(!is.na(Metadata_209_cats$SBP))

##remove rows from cats recieving antihypertensives

#8 rows with cats receiving amlodipine
amlodipine_cases<-Metadata_SBP[c(226, 227, 228, 229, 293, 601, 623, 842),]
print(amlodipine_cases)
##     Cat_ID_timepoint Cat_ID Owner_ID timepoint       date        dob Age_years
## 226         Cat038_4 Cat038   Own031       4.0 12/10/2021 05/10/2007  14.02053
## 227       Cat038_4.5 Cat038   Own031       4.5 10/05/2022 05/10/2007  14.59548
## 228         Cat038_5 Cat038   Own031       5.0 22/11/2022 05/10/2007  15.13210
## 229       Cat038_5.5 Cat038   Own031       5.5 23/05/2023 05/10/2007  15.63039
## 293         Cat045_6 Cat045   Own037       6.0 10/07/2023 08/04/2009  14.25325
## 601       Cat092_5.5 Cat092   Own075       5.5 27/09/2023 20/02/2009  14.59822
## 623       Cat094_5.5 Cat094   Own075       5.5 27/09/2023 01/02/2009  14.65024
## 842       Cat140_4.5 Cat140   Own110       4.5 20/10/2023 01/09/2011  12.13415

## 623 Non-pedigree     DSH   Male           Yes   3.89   4           10
## 842 Non-pedigree     DSH Female           Yes   4.11   4           10

#8 visits from 5 cats in total. 

#remove these from SBP data

Metadata_SBP2<-Metadata_SBP[c(-226, -227, -228, -229, -293, -601, -623, -842),]

#write csv

#write.csv(Metadata_SBP2, "Metadata_SBP2.csv")

#5. summarise data

##a. no.of visits and no.of cats

## [1] 1116   40
## # A tibble: 203 × 2
##    Cat_ID `n_distinct(Cat_ID)`
##    <chr>                 <int>
##  1 Cat001                    1
##  2 Cat002                    1
##  3 Cat003                    1
##  4 Cat004                    1
##  5 Cat005                    1
##  6 Cat006                    1
##  7 Cat007                    1
##  8 Cat008                    1
##  9 Cat009                    1
## 10 Cat010                    1
## # ℹ 193 more rows

##b. summarise age data

# Function to summarise age stats
summarise_age <- function(data, group_var) {
  data %>%
    group_by({{ group_var }}) %>%
    summarise(
      N = n_distinct(Cat_ID),  # use your unique cat ID column
      Median_Age = round(median(Age_years, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
      IQR_Age = paste0(round(quantile(Age_years, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE), 1), "–", 
                       round(quantile(Age_years, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE), 1)),
      Age_Range = paste0(round(min(Age_years, na.rm = TRUE), 1), "–", 
                         round(max(Age_years, na.rm = TRUE), 1)),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}

# Summary tables
summarise_age(Metadata_SBP2)
## # A tibble: 1 × 4
##       N Median_Age IQR_Age  Age_Range
##   <int>      <dbl> <chr>    <chr>    
## 1   203       10.3 8.8–11.9 6.7–16.4
age_by_sex <- summarise_age(Metadata_SBP2, Sex)

age_by_health <- summarise_age(Metadata_SBP2, Overall_health_status)

age_by_breed <- summarise_age(Metadata_SBP2, Breed)

# Combine for full table (optional)
full_age_summary <- bind_rows(
  age_by_sex %>% mutate(Group = paste0("Sex: ", Sex)) %>% select(Group, everything(), -Sex),
  age_by_health %>% mutate(Group = paste0("Health: ", Overall_health_status)) %>% select(Group, everything(), -Overall_health_status),
  age_by_breed %>% mutate(Group = paste0("Breed: ", Breed)) %>% select(Group, everything(), -Breed)
)

# View full summary
print(full_age_summary)
## # A tibble: 6 × 5
##   Group                   N Median_Age IQR_Age  Age_Range
##   <chr>               <int>      <dbl> <chr>    <chr>    
## 1 Sex: Female           106       10.2 8.8–11.9 6.7–16   
## 2 Sex: Male              97       10.3 9–11.9   6.9–16.4 
## 3 Health: Healthy        69        9.5 8.2–10.8 6.9–15.4 
## 4 Health: Not_Healthy   134       10.5 9.1–12.2 6.7–16.4 
## 5 Breed: Non-pedigree   175       10.4 9–12     6.7–16.4 
## 6 Breed: Pedigree        28        9.7 8.4–11.5 7–15.5
# Summary tables for SBP

age_by_sex <- summarise_age(Metadata_SBP2, Sex)

age_by_health <- summarise_age(Metadata_SBP2, Overall_health_status)

age_by_breed <- summarise_age(Metadata_SBP2, Breed)

# Combine for full table (optional)
full_age_summary <- bind_rows(
  age_by_sex %>% mutate(Group = paste0("Sex: ", Sex)) %>% select(Group, everything(), -Sex),
  age_by_health %>% mutate(Group = paste0("Health: ", Overall_health_status)) %>% select(Group, everything(), -Overall_health_status),
  age_by_breed %>% mutate(Group = paste0("Breed: ", Breed)) %>% select(Group, everything(), -Breed)
)

# View full summary
print(full_age_summary)
## # A tibble: 6 × 5
##   Group                   N Median_Age IQR_Age  Age_Range
##   <chr>               <int>      <dbl> <chr>    <chr>    
## 1 Sex: Female           106       10.2 8.8–11.9 6.7–16   
## 2 Sex: Male              97       10.3 9–11.9   6.9–16.4 
## 3 Health: Healthy        69        9.5 8.2–10.8 6.9–15.4 
## 4 Health: Not_Healthy   134       10.5 9.1–12.2 6.7–16.4 
## 5 Breed: Non-pedigree   175       10.4 9–12     6.7–16.4 
## 6 Breed: Pedigree        28        9.7 8.4–11.5 7–15.5
# Combine for full table (optional)
full_age_summary <- bind_rows(
  age_by_sex %>% mutate(Group = paste0("Sex: ", Sex)) %>% select(Group, everything(), -Sex),
  age_by_health %>% mutate(Group = paste0("Health: ", Overall_health_status)) %>% select(Group, everything(), -Overall_health_status),
  age_by_breed %>% mutate(Group = paste0("Breed: ", Breed)) %>% select(Group, everything(), -Breed)
)

# View full summary
print(full_age_summary)
## # A tibble: 6 × 5
##   Group                   N Median_Age IQR_Age  Age_Range
##   <chr>               <int>      <dbl> <chr>    <chr>    
## 1 Sex: Female           106       10.2 8.8–11.9 6.7–16   
## 2 Sex: Male              97       10.3 9–11.9   6.9–16.4 
## 3 Health: Healthy        69        9.5 8.2–10.8 6.9–15.4 
## 4 Health: Not_Healthy   134       10.5 9.1–12.2 6.7–16.4 
## 5 Breed: Non-pedigree   175       10.4 9–12     6.7–16.4 
## 6 Breed: Pedigree        28        9.7 8.4–11.5 7–15.5

##c. ridge plots

# Plot A: Age by Sex
pA <- ggplot(Metadata_SBP2, aes(x = Age_years, y = Sex, fill = Sex)) +
  geom_density_ridges(alpha = 0.7, scale = 1.2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "A. Age distribution by Sex", x = "Age (years)", y = "") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# Plot B: Age by Breed
pB <- ggplot(Metadata_SBP2, aes(x = Age_years, y = Breed, fill = Breed)) +
  geom_density_ridges(alpha = 0.7, scale = 1.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("Pedigree" = "yellow", "Non-pedigree" = "green"))+
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "B. Age distribution by Breed", x = "Age (years)", y = "") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# Plot C: Age by Health Status
#pC <- ggplot(Metadata_SBP2, aes(x = Age_years, y = #Overall_health_status, fill = Overall_health_status)) +
#  geom_density_ridges(alpha = 0.7, scale = 1.2) +
#  theme_minimal() +
#  labs(title = "C. Age distribution by Overall Health Status", #x = "Age (years)", y = "") +
#  theme(legend.position = "none")

# Plot C: Sex by Breed
pC <- ggplot(Metadata_SBP2, aes(x = Sex, y = Breed, fill = Breed)) +
  geom_density_ridges(alpha = 0.7, scale = 1.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("Pedigree" = "yellow", "Non-pedigree" = "green"))+
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "C. Sex distribution by Breed", x = "Sex", y = "") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# Plot D: Age by Sex and Breed
pD <- ggplot(Metadata_SBP2, aes(x = Age_years, fill = Sex)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ Breed) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "D. Age distribution by Sex and Breed", x = "Age (years)", y = "Density")

# Plot F: Age by Sex and Health Status
pF <- ggplot(Metadata_SBP2, aes(x = Age_years, fill = Sex)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ Overall_health_status) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "F. Age distribution by Sex and Health Status", x = "Age (years)", y = "Density")

# Plot G: Age by Breed and Health Status
pG<-ggplot(Metadata_SBP2, aes(x = Age_years, fill = Breed)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ Overall_health_status) + 
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "G. Age Distribution by Breed and Health Status",
       x = "Age (years)", y = "Density")

# Combine using patchwork
(pA + pB) / (pC+ pD) +
  plot_annotation(title = "Distribution of Age, Sex and Breed",
                  theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16)))
## Picking joint bandwidth of 0.529
## Picking joint bandwidth of 0.567
## Picking joint bandwidth of 0.479

Data distribution by age, sex, breed and health status 

#ggsave("Supplementary_Figure_DistributionPlots2.png", #width = 12, height = 12, dpi = 300)

#6. Analysis of SBP

##a. Summarise data

# Count the number of visits with sbpdata
num_visits_with_SBP <- Metadata_SBP2 %>%
  filter(!is.na(SBP)) %>%
  summarise(num_visits = n())

# Count the number of cats with sbp data
num_cats_with_SBP <- Metadata_SBP2 %>%
  filter(!is.na(SBP)) %>%
  summarise(num_cats = n_distinct(Cat_ID))

# Print the results
print(num_visits_with_SBP)
##   num_visits
## 1       1116
print(num_cats_with_SBP)
##   num_cats
## 1      203
# Count the number of visits per cat with sbp data
visits_per_cat <- Metadata_SBP2 %>%
  filter(!is.na(SBP)) %>%
  group_by(Cat_ID) %>%
  summarise(num_visits = n())

# Calculate median, IQR, and range
median_visits <- median(visits_per_cat$num_visits)
iqr_visits <- IQR(visits_per_cat$num_visits)
q1 <- quantile(visits_per_cat$num_visits, 0.25)  # First quartile (Q1)
q3 <- quantile(visits_per_cat$num_visits, 0.75)  # Third quartile (Q3)
range_visits <- range(visits_per_cat$num_visits)  # Returns min and max

# Print the results
print(median_visits)
## [1] 5
print(iqr_visits)
## [1] 6
print(q1)
## 25% 
##   2
print(q3)
## 75% 
##   8
print(range_visits)
## [1]  1 12

##b. Is the SBP data normal?

hist(Metadata_SBP2$SBP, breaks=50)

qqnorm(Metadata_SBP2$SBP)
qqline(Metadata_SBP2$SBP)

shapiro.test(Metadata_SBP2$SBP)
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  Metadata_SBP2$SBP
## W = 0.9632, p-value = 3.504e-16
##data is not normal

##c. describe age at baseline (e.g. first SBP measured) ###i. create df of baseline data

#create dataset of baseline data 

Metadata_SBP2_baseline<-Metadata_SBP2 %>%
  arrange(Cat_ID, date) %>%
  group_by(Cat_ID) %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  ungroup()

###ii. check normality

#is it normal? 
hist(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$SBP, breaks=50)

qqnorm(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$SBP)
qqline(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$SBP)

shapiro.test(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$SBP)
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  Metadata_SBP2_baseline$SBP
## W = 0.9699, p-value = 0.0002432
#not normal

###iii.summarise baseline data

#summarise age
summarise_age(Metadata_SBP2_baseline)
## # A tibble: 1 × 4
##       N Median_Age IQR_Age  Age_Range
##   <int>      <dbl> <chr>    <chr>    
## 1   203        9.8 8.4–11.1 6.7–15.3
#average years follow up per cat
# Calculate follow-up duration per cat
followup_per_cat <- Metadata_SBP2 %>%
  group_by(Cat_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    followup_years = max(Age_years, na.rm = TRUE) - min(Age_years, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

print(followup_per_cat)
## # A tibble: 203 × 2
##    Cat_ID followup_years
##    <chr>           <dbl>
##  1 Cat001           3.93
##  2 Cat002           6.88
##  3 Cat003           0   
##  4 Cat004           0   
##  5 Cat005           5.65
##  6 Cat006           2.61
##  7 Cat007           4.66
##  8 Cat008           6.19
##  9 Cat009           0   
## 10 Cat010           0   
## # ℹ 193 more rows
hist(followup_per_cat$followup_years, breaks=10)

shapiro.test(followup_per_cat$followup_years)
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  followup_per_cat$followup_years
## W = 0.90923, p-value = 8.201e-10
#not normal - median IQR range
# Calculate average follow-up duration across all cats
median_followup <- median(followup_per_cat$followup_years, na.rm = TRUE)
Q1_followup<- quantile(followup_per_cat$followup_years, 0.25)
Q3_followup<- quantile(followup_per_cat$followup_years, 0.75)
min_followup<- min(followup_per_cat$followup_years)
max_followup<- max(followup_per_cat$followup_years)

# Print results
print(followup_per_cat)       # follow-up per cat
## # A tibble: 203 × 2
##    Cat_ID followup_years
##    <chr>           <dbl>
##  1 Cat001           3.93
##  2 Cat002           6.88
##  3 Cat003           0   
##  4 Cat004           0   
##  5 Cat005           5.65
##  6 Cat006           2.61
##  7 Cat007           4.66
##  8 Cat008           6.19
##  9 Cat009           0   
## 10 Cat010           0   
## # ℹ 193 more rows
cat("Median follow-up time (years):", median_followup, "\n")
## Median follow-up time (years): 3.526352
cat("Q1 follow-up time (years):", Q1_followup, "\n")
## Q1 follow-up time (years): 0.973306
cat("Q3 follow-up time (years):", Q3_followup, "\n")
## Q3 follow-up time (years): 5.037645
cat("min follow-up time (years):", min_followup, "\n")
## min follow-up time (years): 0
cat("max follow-up time (years):", max_followup, "\n")
## max follow-up time (years): 6.880219
median(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$SBP, na.rm=T)
## [1] 132
range(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$SBP, na.rm=T)
## [1]  90 195
IQR(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$SBP, na.rm=T)
## [1] 24.8
quantile(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$SBP, 0.25, na.rm=T)
##    25% 
## 119.45
quantile(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$SBP, 0.75, na.rm=T)
##    75% 
## 144.25
median(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$weight, na.rm=T)
## [1] 4.7
range(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$weight, na.rm=T)
## [1] 2.40 9.18
IQR(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$weight, na.rm=T)
## [1] 1.545
quantile(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$weight, 0.25, na.rm=T)
##   25% 
## 3.845
quantile(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$weight, 0.75, na.rm=T)
##  75% 
## 5.39
median(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$BCS, na.rm=T)
## [1] 6
range(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$BCS, na.rm=T)
## [1] 2 9
IQR(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$BCS, na.rm=T)
## [1] 2
quantile(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$BCS, 0.25, na.rm=T)
## 25% 
##   5
quantile(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$BCS, 0.75, na.rm=T)
## 75% 
##   7
median(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$MCS_combined, na.rm=T)
## [1] 30
range(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$MCS_combined, na.rm=T)
## [1] 10 30
IQR(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$MCS_combined, na.rm=T)
## [1] 5
quantile(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$MCS_combined, 0.25, na.rm=T)
## 25% 
##  25
quantile(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$MCS_combined, 0.75, na.rm=T)
## 75% 
##  30
#breed
sum(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$Breed=="Pedigree")
## [1] 28
sum(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$Breed!="Pedigree")
## [1] 175
Metadata_SBP2_baseline%>%
  group_by(breed)%>%
  summarise(N=n())
## # A tibble: 17 × 2
##    breed                  N
##    <chr>              <int>
##  1 American Exotic        2
##  2 Balinese               1
##  3 Bengal                 3
##  4 British short hair     3
##  5 DLH                   21
##  6 DMH                   24
##  7 DSH                  130
##  8 Egyptian Mau           1
##  9 Maine coon             1
## 10 Ocicat                 1
## 11 Oriental               2
## 12 Persian                4
## 13 Ragdoll                4
## 14 Russian blue           1
## 15 Siamese                3
## 16 Somali                 1
## 17 Sphynx                 1
#sex
sum(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$Sex=="Male")
## [1] 97
sum(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$Sex=="Female")
## [1] 106
#neuter
sum(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$neuter_status=="No")
## [1] 2
#morbidities at baseline
sum(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$CKD=="Yes")
## [1] NA
sum(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$Hypertension=="Yes")
## [1] 21
sum(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$Diabetes_mellitus=="Yes")
## [1] 2
sum(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$Hyperthyroidism=="Yes")
## [1] 11
sum(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$oe.abnormalities_Yes_no_incomplete=="Yes", na.rm=TRUE)
## [1] 103
sum(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$Dental_disease=="Yes")
## [1] NA
sum(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$Neoplasia=="Yes")
## [1] NA
sum(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$FIV.Y.N=="Yes")
## Warning: Unknown or uninitialised column: `FIV.Y.N`.
## [1] 0
sum(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$Heart_murmur=="Yes")
## [1] 60
#morbidities by study end
sum(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$CKD_over.study=="Yes", na.rm=T)
## [1] 23
sum(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$hypertensive_over.study=="Yes")
## [1] 41
sum(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$Diabetes_mellitus=="Yes")
## [1] 2
sum(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$hyperthyroid_over.study=="Yes")
## [1] 18
sum(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$oe_abnormalities_over_study=="1")
## [1] 172
sum(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$dental_dz_over_study=="1")
## [1] 172
sum(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$neoplasia_over.study=="Yes")
## [1] 18
sum(Metadata_SBP2_baseline$HM_at_least_once=="Yes")
## [1] 124

##d. plot changes in spahetti plot to see SBP

###i. code for themed plots

My_Theme = theme(
  axis.title.x = element_text(size = 16),
  axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
  axis.text.y = element_text(size=14),
  axis.title.y = element_text(size = 16), 
 plot.title = element_text(size = 16, hjust = 0.5))

###ii. ## age overall

g<-ggplot(Metadata_SBP2, aes(x = Age_years, y = SBP, group = Cat_ID)) +
  geom_line(aes(group=factor(Cat_ID), col=Cat_ID, alpha = 0.5)) +  # Add lines connecting data points for each cat
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +  # Add points for each data point
  labs(x = "Age (years)", y = "Systolic blood pressure (mmHg)", title = "Trends of SBP changes with age in 203 cats") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position="none")

g +My_Theme

#ggsave("Trends of SBP changes with age in 203 cats.png", #plot= last_plot(), bg = "white", height = 2100, width = #3150, units="px" )

###iii. sex

g2<-ggplot(Metadata_SBP2, aes(x = Age_years, y = SBP, group = Cat_ID)) +
  geom_line(aes(group=factor(Cat_ID), col=Sex)) +  # Add lines connecting data points for each cat
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +  # Add points for each data point
  labs(x = "Age (years)", y = "Systolic blood pressure (mmHg)", title = "Trends of SBP changes with age and sex in 203 cats") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position="bottom")

g2 +My_Theme

#ggsave("Trends of SBP changes with age in 203 cats by #sex.png", plot= last_plot(), bg = "white", height = #2100, width = 3150, units="px" )

###iv. facet by Cat_ID

# Split dataframe
cat_ids <- unique(Metadata_SBP2$Cat_ID)

first  <- cat_ids[1:56]
second <- cat_ids[57:112]
third <- cat_ids[113:168]
fourth<- cat_ids[169:203]

ggplot(filter(Metadata_SBP2, Cat_ID %in% first),
             aes(Age_years, SBP, group = Cat_ID)) +
  facet_wrap(~ Cat_ID, ncol = 7) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.5, colour = "steelblue") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, colour = "steelblue") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
## `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation.
## ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic?
## `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation.
## ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic?
## `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation.
## ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic?

## ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic?

## `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation.
## ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic?

## ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic?
## `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation.
## ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic?

ggplot(filter(Metadata_SBP2, Cat_ID %in% second),
             aes(Age_years, SBP, group = Cat_ID)) +
  facet_wrap(~ Cat_ID, ncol = 7) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.5, colour = "steelblue") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, colour = "steelblue") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
## `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation.
## ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic?

             aes(Age_years, SBP, group = Cat_ID)) +
  facet_wrap(~ Cat_ID, ncol = 7) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.5, colour = "steelblue") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, colour = "steelblue") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

  facet_wrap(~ Cat_ID, ncol = 7) +

##e. Linear mixed effects models for SBP changes with age. ###i. model 1 SBP, age, sex ####check splines

#fit model for no splines up to 5df and compare
mod_b1<-lmer(SBP ~ Age_years + Sex + Age_years:Sex +(1|Cat_ID), Metadata_SBP2)
mod_b2<-lmer(SBP ~ ns(Age_years, df=2) + Sex + ns(Age_years, df=2):Sex +(1|Cat_ID), Metadata_SBP2)
mod_b3<-lmer(SBP ~ ns(Age_years, df=3) + Sex + ns(Age_years, df=3):Sex +(1|Cat_ID), Metadata_SBP2)
mod_b4<-lmer(SBP ~ ns(Age_years, df=4) + Sex + ns(Age_years, df=4):Sex +(1|Cat_ID), Metadata_SBP2)
mod_b5<-lmer(SBP ~ ns(Age_years, df=5) + Sex + ns(Age_years, df=5):Sex +(1|Cat_ID), Metadata_SBP2)

#compare
anova(mod_b1, mod_b2, mod_b3, mod_b4, mod_b5)
## refitting model(s) with ML (instead of REML)
## Data: Metadata_SBP2
## Models:
## mod_b1: SBP ~ Age_years + Sex + Age_years:Sex + (1 | Cat_ID)
## mod_b2: SBP ~ ns(Age_years, df = 2) + Sex + ns(Age_years, df = 2):Sex + (1 | Cat_ID)
## mod_b3: SBP ~ ns(Age_years, df = 3) + Sex + ns(Age_years, df = 3):Sex + (1 | Cat_ID)
## mod_b4: SBP ~ ns(Age_years, df = 4) + Sex + ns(Age_years, df = 4):Sex + (1 | Cat_ID)
## mod_b5: SBP ~ ns(Age_years, df = 5) + Sex + ns(Age_years, df = 5):Sex + (1 | Cat_ID)
##        npar    AIC    BIC  logLik -2*log(L)   Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)   
## mod_b1    6 9464.7 9494.8 -4726.3    9452.7                         
## mod_b2    8 9458.3 9498.4 -4721.1    9442.3 10.4054  2   0.005502 **
## mod_b3   10 9458.5 9508.6 -4719.2    9438.5  3.8032  2   0.149326   
## mod_b4   12 9459.5 9519.7 -4717.8    9435.5  2.9554  2   0.228165   
## mod_b5   14 9461.1 9531.3 -4716.5    9433.1  2.4222  2   0.297871   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
#mod_b2 with 2df best

#check model assumptions

check_model(mod_b2)

acf(residuals(mod_b2))##ACF not showing stong autocorrelation. OK to use model. 

#png("check1.png", width = 2000, height = 3000, res = #300)
#print(check1)
#dev.off()

####add owner as nested re

mod_b2_nested<-lmer(SBP ~ ns(Age_years, df=2) + Sex + ns(Age_years, df=2):Sex +(1|Owner_ID/Cat_ID), Metadata_SBP2)
summary(mod_b2_nested)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod']
## Formula: SBP ~ ns(Age_years, df = 2) + Sex + ns(Age_years, df = 2):Sex +  
##     (1 | Owner_ID/Cat_ID)
##    Data: Metadata_SBP2
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 9416.1
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -4.2363 -0.6472 -0.0308  0.5527  5.4690 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups          Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  Cat_ID:Owner_ID (Intercept) 116.77   10.806  
##  Owner_ID        (Intercept)  22.07    4.698  
##  Residual                    216.44   14.712  
## Number of obs: 1116, groups:  Cat_ID:Owner_ID, 203; Owner_ID, 146
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                                Estimate Std. Error t value
## (Intercept)                     116.024      2.429  47.769
## ns(Age_years, df = 2)1           42.539      4.869   8.737
## ns(Age_years, df = 2)2           27.982      4.058   6.896
## SexMale                           8.898      3.473   2.562
## ns(Age_years, df = 2)1:SexMale    2.762      6.898   0.400
## ns(Age_years, df = 2)2:SexMale   23.008      5.845   3.937
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) ns(A_,d=2)1 ns(A_,d=2)2 SexMal n(A_,d=2)1:
## ns(A_,d=2)1 -0.797                                           
## ns(A_,d=2)2  0.095  0.124                                    
## SexMale     -0.680  0.551      -0.069                        
## n(A_,d=2)1:  0.560 -0.705      -0.087      -0.801            
## n(A_,d=2)2: -0.067 -0.086      -0.695       0.115  0.111
tab_model(mod_b2_nested)
  SBP
Predictors Estimates CI p
(Intercept) 116.02 111.26 – 120.79 <0.001
Age years [1st degree] 42.54 32.99 – 52.09 <0.001
Age years [2nd degree] 27.98 20.02 – 35.94 <0.001
Sex [Male] 8.90 2.08 – 15.71 0.011
Age years [1st degree] ×
Sex [Male] 		2.76 -10.77 – 16.30 0.689
Age years [2nd degree] ×
Sex [Male] 		23.01 11.54 – 34.48 <0.001
Random Effects
σ2 216.44
τ00 Cat_ID:Owner_ID 116.77
τ00 Owner_ID 22.07
ICC 0.39
N Cat_ID 203
N Owner_ID 146
Observations 1116
Marginal R2 / Conditional R2 0.232 / 0.532 
anova(mod_b2, mod_b2_nested)
## refitting model(s) with ML (instead of REML)
## Data: Metadata_SBP2
## Models:
## mod_b2: SBP ~ ns(Age_years, df = 2) + Sex + ns(Age_years, df = 2):Sex + (1 | Cat_ID)
## mod_b2_nested: SBP ~ ns(Age_years, df = 2) + Sex + ns(Age_years, df = 2):Sex + (1 | Owner_ID/Cat_ID)
##               npar    AIC    BIC  logLik -2*log(L)  Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)
## mod_b2           8 9458.3 9498.4 -4721.1    9442.3                     
## mod_b2_nested    9 9458.5 9503.7 -4720.2    9440.5 1.7768  1     0.1825

####check mod1 assumptions

check1<-check_model(mod_b2_nested)
print(check1)

png("check1.png", width = 2000, height = 3000, res = 300)
print(check1)
dev.off()
## png 
##   2
# Extract residuals
res <- residuals(mod_b2_nested)
#qq plot of resids
ggplot(data.frame(res), aes(sample = res)) +
  stat_qq() +
  stat_qq_line(color = "red") +
  theme_bw()

#histogram of resids
hist(res, breaks = 20, main = "Residuals", xlab = "Residuals")

#variance of random effects
VarCorr(mod_b2_nested)
##  Groups          Name        Std.Dev.
##  Cat_ID:Owner_ID (Intercept) 10.8058 
##  Owner_ID        (Intercept)  4.6981 
##  Residual                    14.7118

####compare overall effect of age with LRT

mod_b2_noage<-lmer(SBP ~  Sex +  +(1|Owner_ID/Cat_ID), Metadata_SBP2)

anova(mod_b2_noage, mod_b2_nested)
## refitting model(s) with ML (instead of REML)
## Data: Metadata_SBP2
## Models:
## mod_b2_noage: SBP ~ Sex + +(1 | Owner_ID/Cat_ID)
## mod_b2_nested: SBP ~ ns(Age_years, df = 2) + Sex + ns(Age_years, df = 2):Sex + (1 | Owner_ID/Cat_ID)
##               npar    AIC    BIC  logLik -2*log(L)  Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## mod_b2_noage     5 9728.8 9753.9 -4859.4    9718.8                         
## mod_b2_nested    9 9458.5 9503.7 -4720.2    9440.5 278.35  4  < 2.2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
#mod with age significantly improves model fit P<0.001

###ii. model2: sbp, age, sex, diseases ####ensure data ordered correctly

Metadata_SBP2 <- Metadata_SBP2 %>%
  mutate(MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe = case_when(
    MCS_combined == 30 ~ "None",
    MCS_combined %in% 20:29 ~ "Mild",
    MCS_combined %in% 0:19 ~ "Moderate to severe",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  ))

Metadata_SBP2$MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe<-factor(Metadata_SBP2$MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe, levels= c( "None", "Mild", "Moderate to severe"))

#####arrange BCS into categories

Metadata_SBP2 <- Metadata_SBP2 %>%
  mutate(BCS_group3 = case_when(
    BCS %in% 1:4 ~ "1-4",
    BCS == 5     ~ "5",
    BCS == 6     ~ "6",
    BCS %in% 7:9 ~ "7-9",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  ))
#set ref category as 5 
Metadata_SBP2  <- Metadata_SBP2  %>%
  mutate(BCS_group3 = factor(
    BCS_group3,
    levels = c("5", "1-4", "6", "7-9")
  ))
# Count cats in each category
cat_counts <- Metadata_SBP2  %>%
  count(BCS_group3)
cat_counts 
##   BCS_group3   n
## 1          5 308
## 2        1-4 143
## 3          6 267
## 4        7-9 392
## 5       <NA>   6
group HM
Metadata_SBP2<-Metadata_SBP2%>%
  mutate(
    HM_group = case_when(
      HM_grade == 0 ~ "None", 
      HM_grade %in% c(1,2) ~ "I-II/VI",
      HM_grade >=3 ~ "≥III/VI",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ))
#    
levels(Metadata_SBP2$HM_group)
## NULL
#
as.factor(Metadata_SBP2$HM_group)  
##    [1] ≥III/VI None    ≥III/VI ≥III/VI I-II/VI I-II/VI None    None    None   
##   [10] None    None    None    None    None    None    I-II/VI None    None   
##   [19] None    None    None    None    I-II/VI None    None    None    I-II/VI
##   [28] None    None    None    None    None    None    None    None    None   
##   [37] ≥III/VI I-II/VI I-II/VI I-II/VI I-II/VI None    None    None    I-II/VI
##   [46] I-II/VI ≥III/VI ≥III/VI I-II/VI None    None    None    None    None   
##   [55] None    None    None    None    I-II/VI None    None    None    None   
##   [64] None    None    None    None    None    None    None    None    I-II/VI
##   [73] I-II/VI None    ≥III/VI ≥III/VI I-II/VI ≥III/VI None    None    None

##   [91] None    None    None    None    None    None    None    None    None

##  [514] None    None    None    None    None    ≥III/VI None    ≥III/VI ≥III/VI
##  [523] ≥III/VI I-II/VI I-II/VI ≥III/VI <NA>    None    None    None    I-II/VI
##  [532] None    None    None    None    None    I-II/VI I-II/VI ≥III/VI ≥III/VI
##  [541] I-II/VI ≥III/VI ≥III/VI None    None    None    None    I-II/VI None   
##  [550] None    I-II/VI I-II/VI None    None    ≥III/VI None    None    None

##  [964] I-II/VI None    I-II/VI I-II/VI I-II/VI None    I-II/VI I-II/VI None   
##  [973] None    I-II/VI None    I-II/VI None    None    None    None    None   
##  [982] None    None    I-II/VI None    I-II/VI None    None    None    None   
##  [991] None    None    I-II/VI None    None    None    None    None    None   
## [1000] None    ≥III/VI ≥III/VI None    ≥III/VI ≥III/VI ≥III/VI I-II/VI None   
## [1009] None    None    <NA>    None    None    None    I-II/VI None    None   
## [1018] None    None    I-II/VI I-II/VI I-II/VI None    I-II/VI ≥III/VI ≥III/VI
## [1027] I-II/VI None    None    None    None    None    None    I-II/VI I-II/VI
## [1036] None    None    None    None    None    None    None    None    None   
## [1045] None    None    None    None    None    I-II/VI None    I-II/VI None   
## [1054] I-II/VI None    None    None    None    None    None    None    I-II/VI
## [1063] I-II/VI None    None    None    None    None    None    None    None   
## [1072] None    None    None    None    None    None    I-II/VI None    None   
## [1081] None    I-II/VI I-II/VI I-II/VI I-II/VI I-II/VI I-II/VI I-II/VI None   
## [1090] None    None    None    None    None    I-II/VI None    I-II/VI None   
## [1099] None    I-II/VI I-II/VI I-II/VI I-II/VI None    I-II/VI I-II/VI I-II/VI
## [1108] I-II/VI None    None    None    None    None    None    None    None   
## Levels: ≥III/VI I-II/VI None
#
Metadata_SBP2$HM_group<-factor(Metadata_SBP2$HM_group, levels = c("None", "I-II/VI", "≥III/VI"))

#####group muscle loss

Metadata_SBP2<-Metadata_SBP2%>%
  mutate(
    MCS_binary= case_when(
       MCS_combined == 30 ~ "None", 
       MCS_combined %in% c(0:29) ~ "Muscle loss",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ))
#    
levels(Metadata_SBP2$MCS_binary)
## NULL
#
as.factor(Metadata_SBP2$MCS_binary)

## [1015] <NA>        Muscle loss None        None        Muscle loss Muscle loss
## [1021] Muscle loss Muscle loss None        None        Muscle loss None       
## [1027] Muscle loss None        Muscle loss <NA>        None        Muscle loss
## [1033] Muscle loss None        Muscle loss None        Muscle loss Muscle loss
## [1039] Muscle loss None        Muscle loss None        Muscle loss None       
## [1045] None        Muscle loss Muscle loss None        None        Muscle loss

## [1057] None        Muscle loss Muscle loss Muscle loss None        Muscle loss
## [1063] None        Muscle loss None        None        None        None       
## [1069] None        Muscle loss None        None        None        <NA>       
## [1075] Muscle loss None        None        Muscle loss Muscle loss Muscle loss
## [1081] Muscle loss Muscle loss Muscle loss Muscle loss Muscle loss Muscle loss
## [1087] Muscle loss Muscle loss None        Muscle loss Muscle loss Muscle loss
## [1093] Muscle loss <NA>        None        None        <NA>        None

## [1105] Muscle loss None        Muscle loss Muscle loss None        None       
## [1111] None        None        <NA>        Muscle loss Muscle loss None       
## Levels: Muscle loss None
#
Metadata_SBP2$MCS_binary<-factor(Metadata_SBP2$MCS_binary, levels = c("None", "Muscle loss"))

# Count cats in each category
cat_counts <- Metadata_SBP2  %>%
  count(MCS_binary)
cat_counts 
##    MCS_binary   n
## 1        None 509
## 2 Muscle loss 557
## 3        <NA>  50

####run model

mod_bc <- lmer(SBP ~ ns(Age_years, df = 2) + Sex + ns(Age_years, df=2):Sex + Breed + BCS_group3 + MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe +
                 CKD + Hyperthyroidism +  Neoplasia + 
                 Heart_murmur + Diabetes_mellitus + Dental_disease + oe.abnormalities_Yes_no_incomplete +
                 (1 | Cat_ID), data = Metadata_SBP2)

summary(mod_bc)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod']
## Formula: SBP ~ ns(Age_years, df = 2) + Sex + ns(Age_years, df = 2):Sex +  
##     Breed + BCS_group3 + MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe +  
##     CKD + Hyperthyroidism + Neoplasia + Heart_murmur + Diabetes_mellitus +  
##     Dental_disease + oe.abnormalities_Yes_no_incomplete + (1 |      Cat_ID)
##    Data: Metadata_SBP2
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 8137.5
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.6430 -0.6426 -0.0251  0.5460  5.6600 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  Cat_ID   (Intercept) 130.6    11.43   
##  Residual             206.6    14.38   
## Number of obs: 976, groups:  Cat_ID, 199
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                                                          Estimate Std. Error
## (Intercept)                                              112.2382     2.9066
## ns(Age_years, df = 2)1                                    33.6093     5.4416
## ns(Age_years, df = 2)2                                    19.7707     4.7767
## SexMale                                                    9.7005     3.5480
## BreedPedigree                                              4.0659     2.8766
## BCS_group31-4                                             -2.3629     1.9465
## BCS_group36                                                2.9250     1.5487
## BCS_group37-9                                              6.1532     1.6716
## MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_SevereMild                 4.9895     1.3048
## MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_SevereModerate to severe   7.0985     3.0094
## CKDYes                                                     4.7327     2.7974
## HyperthyroidismYes                                        11.2173     2.8629
## NeoplasiaYes                                             -18.7080     5.9464
## Heart_murmurYes                                            0.6048     1.2681
## Diabetes_mellitusYes                                      -6.0730     6.5519
## Dental_diseaseYes                                          1.4326     1.1039
## oe.abnormalities_Yes_no_incompleteIncomplete_OE           -0.4639     1.8166
## oe.abnormalities_Yes_no_incompleteYes                      0.5048     1.6997
## ns(Age_years, df = 2)1:SexMale                            -0.7438     7.1396
## ns(Age_years, df = 2)2:SexMale                            23.4843     6.0832
##                                                          t value
## (Intercept)                                               38.615
## ns(Age_years, df = 2)1                                     6.176
## ns(Age_years, df = 2)2                                     4.139
## SexMale                                                    2.734
## BreedPedigree                                              1.413
## BCS_group31-4                                             -1.214
## BCS_group36                                                1.889
## BCS_group37-9                                              3.681
## MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_SevereMild                 3.824
## MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_SevereModerate to severe   2.359
## CKDYes                                                     1.692
## HyperthyroidismYes                                         3.918
## NeoplasiaYes                                              -3.146
## Heart_murmurYes                                            0.477
## Diabetes_mellitusYes                                      -0.927
## Dental_diseaseYes                                          1.298
## oe.abnormalities_Yes_no_incompleteIncomplete_OE           -0.255
## oe.abnormalities_Yes_no_incompleteYes                      0.297
## ns(Age_years, df = 2)1:SexMale                            -0.104
## ns(Age_years, df = 2)2:SexMale                             3.861
## 
## Correlation matrix not shown by default, as p = 20 > 12.
## Use print(x, correlation=TRUE)  or
##     vcov(x)        if you need it

#####check N per disease in this model

Metadata_mod_bc<- Metadata_SBP2 %>%
  select(Cat_ID, Cat_ID_timepoint, Sex, BCS_group, MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe, SBP, CKD, Hyperthyroidism, 
         Neoplasia, Heart_murmur, Diabetes_mellitus, 
         Dental_disease, oe.abnormalities_Yes_no_incomplete)
#remove NAs
Metadata_mod_bc<-na.omit(Metadata_mod_bc)

distinct_cats_per_covariate_mod_bc <- Metadata_mod_bc %>%
  summarise(
    BCS_underweight=n_distinct(Cat_ID[BCS_group == "Underweight"]), 
     BCS_ideal=n_distinct(Cat_ID[BCS_group == "Ideal weight"]), 
    BCS_overweight=n_distinct(Cat_ID[BCS_group == "Overweight"]), 
    BCS_obese=n_distinct(Cat_ID[BCS_group == "Obese"]), 
    MCS_none=n_distinct(Cat_ID[MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe == "None"]), 
    MCS_mild=n_distinct(Cat_ID[MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe == "Mild"]), 
    MCS_mod_to_severe=n_distinct(Cat_ID[MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe == "Moderate_to_severe"]), 
    CKD = n_distinct(Cat_ID[CKD == "Yes"]),
    Hyperthyroidism = n_distinct(Cat_ID[Hyperthyroidism == "Yes"]),
    Neoplasia = n_distinct(Cat_ID[Neoplasia == "Yes"]),
    Heart_murmur = n_distinct(Cat_ID[Heart_murmur == "Yes"]),
    diabetes = n_distinct(Cat_ID[Diabetes_mellitus == "Yes"]),
    Dental_disease = n_distinct(Cat_ID[Dental_disease == "Yes"]),
    oe_abnormalities_Yes = n_distinct(Cat_ID[oe.abnormalities_Yes_no_incomplete =="Yes"]),
    oe_abnormalities_unable = n_distinct(Cat_ID[oe.abnormalities_Yes_no_incomplete =="Incomplete_OE"]),
     Sex = n_distinct(Cat_ID[Sex == "Male"])
  )

#Print the number of distinct cats that meet the conditions
print(distinct_cats_per_covariate_mod_bc)

tbl_regression(mod_bc, exp=TRUE)%>%
  add_n(location="level")

tbl_regression(mod_bc2, exp=TRUE)%>%
  add_n(location="level")

tbl_regression(mod_bb1, exp=TRUE)%>%
  add_n(location="level")

#####owner nested

mod_bc_nested <- lmer(SBP ~ ns(Age_years, df = 2) + Sex + ns(Age_years, df=2):Sex + Breed + BCS_group3 + MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe +
                 CKD + Hyperthyroidism +  Neoplasia +
                 HM_group + HR + Diabetes_mellitus + Dental_disease  + (1 | Owner_ID/Cat_ID), data = Metadata_SBP2)

tab_model(mod_bc_nested)
  SBP
Predictors Estimates CI p
(Intercept) 100.54 89.82 – 111.26 <0.001

BCS_group37-9 5.34 2.18 – 8.51 0.001

#####compare splines

mod_bc_nested2 <- lmer(SBP ~ Age_years + Sex + Age_years:Sex + Breed + BCS_group3 + MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe +
                 CKD + Hyperthyroidism +  Neoplasia +
                 HM_group + HR + Diabetes_mellitus + Dental_disease  + (1 | Owner_ID/Cat_ID), data = Metadata_SBP2)


anova(mod_bc_nested, mod_bc_nested2)#splines significantly improve model 
## refitting model(s) with ML (instead of REML)
## Data: Metadata_SBP2
## Models:
## mod_bc_nested2: SBP ~ Age_years + Sex + Age_years:Sex + Breed + BCS_group3 + MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe + CKD + Hyperthyroidism + Neoplasia + HM_group + HR + Diabetes_mellitus + Dental_disease + (1 | Owner_ID/Cat_ID)
## mod_bc_nested: SBP ~ ns(Age_years, df = 2) + Sex + ns(Age_years, df = 2):Sex + Breed + BCS_group3 + MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe + CKD + Hyperthyroidism + Neoplasia + HM_group + HR + Diabetes_mellitus + Dental_disease + (1 | Owner_ID/Cat_ID)
##                npar    AIC    BIC  logLik -2*log(L)  Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)   
## mod_bc_nested2   21 8637.3 8740.8 -4297.7    8595.3                        
## mod_bc_nested    23 8629.0 8742.4 -4291.5    8583.0 12.306  2   0.002128 **
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
AIC(mod_bc_nested, mod_bc_nested2)
##                df      AIC
## mod_bc_nested  23 8565.854
## mod_bc_nested2 21 8593.827

#####check mod 2 assumptions

check2<-check_model(mod_bc_nested2)
print(check2)

check_collinearity(mod_bc_nested2)
## Model has interaction terms. VIFs might be inflated.
##   Try to center the variables used for the interaction, or check
##   multicollinearity among predictors of a model without interaction terms.
## # Check for Multicollinearity
## 
## Low Correlation
## 
##                                    Term  VIF    VIF 95% CI adj. VIF Tolerance
##                               Age_years 2.76 [2.51,  3.05]     1.66      0.36
##                                   Breed 1.03 [1.00,  1.25]     1.01      0.97
##                              BCS_group3 1.31 [1.23,  1.43]     1.15      0.76
##  MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe 1.97 [1.81,  2.17]     1.40      0.51
##                                     CKD 1.05 [1.01,  1.19]     1.03      0.95
##                         Hyperthyroidism 1.14 [1.08,  1.24]     1.07      0.88
##                               Neoplasia 1.09 [1.04,  1.19]     1.04      0.92
##                                HM_group 1.11 [1.06,  1.21]     1.05      0.90
##                                      HR 1.04 [1.01,  1.20]     1.02      0.96
##                       Diabetes_mellitus 1.02 [1.00,  1.36]     1.01      0.98
##                          Dental_disease 1.01 [1.00,  1.96]     1.01      0.99
##  Tolerance 95% CI
##      [0.33, 0.40]
##      [0.80, 1.00]
##      [0.70, 0.81]
##      [0.46, 0.55]
##      [0.84, 0.99]
##      [0.80, 0.93]
##      [0.84, 0.96]
##      [0.82, 0.95]
##      [0.83, 0.99]
##      [0.74, 1.00]
##      [0.51, 1.00]
## 
## Moderate Correlation
## 
##  Term  VIF    VIF 95% CI adj. VIF Tolerance Tolerance 95% CI
##   Sex 9.28 [8.29, 10.40]     3.05      0.11     [0.10, 0.12]
## 
## High Correlation
## 
##           Term   VIF    VIF 95% CI adj. VIF Tolerance Tolerance 95% CI
##  Age_years:Sex 10.50 [9.37, 11.79]     3.24      0.10     [0.08, 0.11]
png("check2.png", width = 2000, height = 3000, res = 300)
print(check2)
dev.off()
## png 
##   2
# Extract residuals
res <- residuals(mod_bc_nested2)
#qq plot of resids
ggplot(data.frame(res), aes(sample = res)) +
  stat_qq() +
  stat_qq_line(color = "red") +
  theme_bw()

#histogram of resids
hist(res, breaks = 20, main = "Residuals", xlab = "Residuals")

#####LRT to compare overall effect of age

mod_bc_noage<- lmer(SBP ~  + Sex + Breed + BCS_group3 + MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe +
                 CKD + Hyperthyroidism +  Neoplasia +
                 HM_group + HR + Diabetes_mellitus + Dental_disease  + (1 | Owner_ID/Cat_ID), data = Metadata_SBP2)

anova(mod_bc_noage, mod_bc_nested)
## refitting model(s) with ML (instead of REML)
## Data: Metadata_SBP2
## Models:
## mod_bc_noage: SBP ~ +Sex + Breed + BCS_group3 + MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe + CKD + Hyperthyroidism + Neoplasia + HM_group + HR + Diabetes_mellitus + Dental_disease + (1 | Owner_ID/Cat_ID)
## mod_bc_nested: SBP ~ ns(Age_years, df = 2) + Sex + ns(Age_years, df = 2):Sex + Breed + BCS_group3 + MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe + CKD + Hyperthyroidism + Neoplasia + HM_group + HR + Diabetes_mellitus + Dental_disease + (1 | Owner_ID/Cat_ID)
##               npar  AIC    BIC  logLik -2*log(L)  Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## mod_bc_noage    19 8732 8825.7 -4347.0      8694                         
## mod_bc_nested   23 8629 8742.4 -4291.5      8583 111.01  4  < 2.2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
#Age significantly improved model P<0.001

##iii. mod 3 subset for OE score

mod_oe_nested <- lmer(SBP ~ ns(Age_years, df = 2) + Sex + ns(Age_years, df=2):Sex + Breed + BCS_group3 + MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe +
                 CKD + Hyperthyroidism +  Neoplasia +
                 HM_group + HR + Diabetes_mellitus + Dental_disease + oe.abnormality_score +  (1 | Owner_ID/Cat_ID), data = Metadata_SBP2)

tab_model(mod_oe_nested)

#####check mod 3 assumptions

check3<-check_model(mod_oe_nested)
print(check3)

check_collinearity(mod_oe_nested)

res <- residuals(mod_oe_nested)
#qq plot of resids
ggplot(data.frame(res), aes(sample = res)) +
  stat_qq() +
  stat_qq_line(color = "red") +
  theme_bw()

#histogram of resids
hist(res, breaks = 20, main = "Residuals", xlab = "Residuals")

##iv. sensitivity analyses ####Now subset by healthy cats only

Metadata_healthy<-Metadata_SBP2%>%
  filter(Healthy_visit=="Yes")

dim(Metadata_healthy)

  filter(Hypertension=="Yes")

## 32                                   Mild          5     None Muscle loss
mod_healthy<-lmer(SBP ~ ns(Age_years, df = 2) + Sex + ns(Age_years, df=2):Sex + (1|Owner_ID/Cat_ID), Metadata_healthy)
mod_healthy2<-lmer(SBP ~ ns(Age_years, df = 3) + Sex + ns(Age_years, df=3):Sex + (1|Owner_ID/Cat_ID), Metadata_healthy)
tab_model(mod_healthy2)
  SBP
Predictors Estimates CI p
(Intercept) 115.22 108.51 – 121.92 <0.001
Age years [1st degree] 14.25 7.48 – 21.02 <0.001
Age years [2nd degree] 30.06 14.40 – 45.72 <0.001
Age years [3rd degree] 16.33 6.35 – 26.30 0.001
Sex [Male] 12.00 2.29 – 21.71 0.016
Age years [1st degree] ×
Sex [Male] 		0.22 -10.32 – 10.75 0.968
Age years [2nd degree] ×
Sex [Male] 		-13.61 -38.10 – 10.88 0.276
Age years [3rd degree] ×
Sex [Male] 		6.41 -13.72 – 26.55 0.532
Random Effects
σ2 184.27
τ00 Cat_ID:Owner_ID 57.00
τ00 Owner_ID 8.99
ICC 0.26
N Cat_ID 170
N Owner_ID 125
Observations 707
Marginal R2 / Conditional R2 0.111 / 0.346 
mod_healthy3<-lmer(SBP ~ Age_years + Sex + Age_years:Sex + (1|Owner_ID/Cat_ID), Metadata_healthy)

anova(mod_healthy, mod_healthy2, mod_healthy3)
## refitting model(s) with ML (instead of REML)
## Data: Metadata_healthy
## Models:
## mod_healthy3: SBP ~ Age_years + Sex + Age_years:Sex + (1 | Owner_ID/Cat_ID)
## mod_healthy: SBP ~ ns(Age_years, df = 2) + Sex + ns(Age_years, df = 2):Sex + (1 | Owner_ID/Cat_ID)
## mod_healthy2: SBP ~ ns(Age_years, df = 3) + Sex + ns(Age_years, df = 3):Sex + (1 | Owner_ID/Cat_ID)
##              npar    AIC    BIC  logLik -2*log(L)  Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)  
## mod_healthy3    7 5850.4 5882.3 -2918.2    5836.4                       
## mod_healthy     9 5849.5 5890.6 -2915.8    5831.5 4.8735  2    0.08745 .
## mod_healthy2   11 5851.8 5901.9 -2914.9    5829.8 1.7705  2    0.41261  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
tab_model(mod_healthy)
  SBP
Predictors Estimates CI p
(Intercept) 116.06 110.98 – 121.13 <0.001
Age years [1st degree] 30.79 20.05 – 41.54 <0.001
Age years [2nd degree] 13.13 4.60 – 21.67 0.003
Sex [Male] 8.35 0.88 – 15.83 0.029
Age years [1st degree] ×
Sex [Male] 		-4.11 -19.63 – 11.42 0.604
Age years [2nd degree] ×
Sex [Male] 		16.51 0.76 – 32.26 0.040
Random Effects
σ2 183.45
τ00 Cat_ID:Owner_ID 57.61
τ00 Owner_ID 10.25
ICC 0.27
N Cat_ID 170
N Owner_ID 125
Observations 707
Marginal R2 / Conditional R2 0.110 / 0.350 
plot_model(mod_healthy, type="pred", terms=c("Age_years[all]", "Sex"), show.data=F)
## Some of the focal terms are of type `character`. This may lead to
##   unexpected results. It is recommended to convert these variables to
##   factors before fitting the model.
##   The following variables are of type character: `Sex`

plot_model(mod_healthy, type="pred", terms=c("Age_years[all]"), show.data=F)

#####check mod assumptions

check4<-check_model(mod_healthy)
print(check4)

check_collinearity(mod_healthy)
## # Check for Multicollinearity
## 
## Low Correlation
## 
##                   Term  VIF   VIF 95% CI adj. VIF Tolerance Tolerance 95% CI
##  ns(Age_years, df = 2) 2.73 [2.43, 3.08]     1.65      0.37     [0.32, 0.41]
## 
## Moderate Correlation
## 
##                       Term  VIF   VIF 95% CI adj. VIF Tolerance
##                        Sex 5.00 [4.40, 5.71]     2.24      0.20
##  ns(Age_years, df = 2):Sex 8.67 [7.57, 9.96]     2.95      0.12
##  Tolerance 95% CI
##      [0.18, 0.23]
##      [0.10, 0.13]
# Extract residuals
res <- residuals(mod_healthy)
#qq plot of resids
ggplot(data.frame(res), aes(sample = res)) +
  stat_qq() +
  stat_qq_line(color = "red") +
  theme_bw()

#histogram of resids
hist(res, breaks = 20, main = "Residuals", xlab = "Residuals")

####subet by “healthy over entire study” interactions

data_healthyoverstudy<-Metadata_SBP2%>%
  filter(Overall_health_status=="Healthy")
  

mod_healthyoverstudy<-lmer(SBP ~ ns(Age_years, df=2) + Sex + ns(Age_years, df=2):Sex +(1|Cat_ID), data_healthyoverstudy)

tab_model(mod_healthyoverstudy)
  SBP
Predictors Estimates CI p
(Intercept) 119.44 113.25 – 125.63 <0.001
Age years [1st degree] 24.80 11.46 – 38.15 <0.001
Age years [2nd degree] 16.11 1.95 – 30.27 0.026
Sex [Male] 5.58 -4.06 – 15.22 0.255
Age years [1st degree] ×
Sex [Male] 		-4.94 -26.16 – 16.29 0.648
Age years [2nd degree] ×
Sex [Male] 		-1.55 -24.56 – 21.46 0.895
Random Effects
σ2 169.01
τ00 Cat_ID 65.81
ICC 0.28
N Cat_ID 69
Observations 276
Marginal R2 / Conditional R2 0.090 / 0.345 
plot_model(mod_healthyoverstudy, type="pred")
## Model contains splines or polynomial terms. Consider using
##   `terms="Age_years [all]"` to get smooth plots. See also package-vignette
##   'Adjusted Predictions at Specific Values'.
## Some of the focal terms are of type `character`. This may lead to
##   unexpected results. It is recommended to convert these variables to
##   factors before fitting the model.
##   The following variables are of type character: `Sex`
## Model contains splines or polynomial terms. Consider using `terms="Sex
##   [all]"` to get smooth plots. See also package-vignette 'Adjusted
##   Predictions at Specific Values'.
## $Age_years

## 
## $Sex

#interactions
mod_int<-lmer(SBP ~ ns(Age_years, df=2) + Sex + ns(Age_years, df=2):Sex + Overall_health_status + ns(Age_years, df=2):Overall_health_status+ (1|Owner_ID/Cat_ID), Metadata_SBP2)

tab_model(mod_int)
  SBP
Predictors Estimates CI p
(Intercept) 116.26 109.72 – 122.79 <0.001
Age years [1st degree] 27.80 14.01 – 41.59 <0.001
Age years [2nd degree] 8.45 -9.79 – 26.69 0.364
Sex [Male] 9.41 2.61 – 16.21 0.007
Overall health status
[Not_Healthy] 		-1.34 -8.62 – 5.94 0.719
Age years [1st degree] ×
Sex [Male] 		0.01 -13.60 – 13.62 0.999
Age years [2nd degree] ×
Sex [Male] 		22.14 10.68 – 33.60 <0.001
Age years [1st degree] ×
Overall health status
[Not_Healthy] 		20.77 5.51 – 36.03 0.008
Age years [2nd degree] ×
Overall health status
[Not_Healthy] 		21.81 3.09 – 40.54 0.022
Random Effects
σ2 215.34
τ00 Cat_ID:Owner_ID 107.05
τ00 Owner_ID 27.65
ICC 0.38
N Cat_ID 203
N Owner_ID 146
Observations 1116
Marginal R2 / Conditional R2 0.250 / 0.538 
plot_model(mod_int, type="emm", terms=c("Age_years", "Overall_health_status"), colors = c("orange", "purple"))+
  xlab("Age (years)") +
  ylab("SBP (mmHg)") +
  ggtitle("") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")+
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(100, 200))
## Some of the focal terms are of type `character`. This may lead to
##   unexpected results. It is recommended to convert these variables to
##   factors before fitting the model.
##   The following variables are of type character: `Overall_health_status`
## Model contains splines or polynomial terms. Consider using
##   `terms="Age_years [all]"` to get smooth plots. See also package-vignette
##   'Adjusted Predictions at Specific Values'.
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

Metadata_SBP2%>% filter (Overall_health_status=="Healthy") %>%   summarise(n_healthy_cats = n_distinct(Cat_ID))
##   n_healthy_cats
## 1             69
#
ggsave("Fig3_age_health_interactions.png", plot= last_plot(), bg = "white", height =10, width = 15, units="cm" )

#####Check mod assumptions

check5<-check_model(mod_int)
png("check5.png", width = 3000, height = 4000, res = 300)
print(check5)
dev.off()
## png 
##   2
check_collinearity(mod_int)
## Model has interaction terms. VIFs might be inflated.
##   Try to center the variables used for the interaction, or check
##   multicollinearity among predictors of a model without interaction terms.
## # Check for Multicollinearity
## 
## Low Correlation
## 
##                   Term  VIF     VIF 95% CI adj. VIF Tolerance Tolerance 95% CI
##                    Sex 3.26 [ 2.96,  3.61]     1.81      0.31     [0.28, 0.34]
##  Overall_health_status 3.12 [ 2.83,  3.44]     1.77      0.32     [0.29, 0.35]
## 
## Moderate Correlation
## 
##                       Term  VIF     VIF 95% CI adj. VIF Tolerance
##  ns(Age_years, df = 2):Sex 8.53 [ 7.66,  9.53]     2.92      0.12
##  Tolerance 95% CI
##      [0.10, 0.13]
## 
## High Correlation
## 
##                                         Term   VIF     VIF 95% CI adj. VIF
##                        ns(Age_years, df = 2) 41.21 [36.75, 46.23]     6.42
##  ns(Age_years, df = 2):Overall_health_status 57.28 [51.06, 64.28]     7.57
##  Tolerance Tolerance 95% CI
##       0.02     [0.02, 0.03]
##       0.02     [0.02, 0.02]
# Extract residuals
res <- residuals(mod_int)
#qq plot of resids
ggplot(data.frame(res), aes(sample = res)) +
  stat_qq() +
  stat_qq_line(color = "red") +
  theme_bw()

#histogram of resids
hist(res, breaks = 20, main = "Residuals", xlab = "Residuals")

##vi. plot of results

pA2<-plot_model(mod_b2_nested, type = "emm", color = "black", terms = c("Age_years[all]")) +
  xlab("Age (years)") +
  ylab("SBP (mmHg)") +
  ggtitle("A") +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(100, 200))
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.
pA2

#ggsave("A SBP and age plot_mod1.png", plot= #last_plot(), bg = "white", height =10, width = 10, #units="cm" )

pB2<-plot_model(mod_b2_nested, type="emm",color=c("#00BFC4", "#F8766D"), terms=c("Age_years[all]", "Sex"))+ 
  xlab("Age (years)")+
  ylab("SBP (mmHg)") + 
  ggtitle("B")+  
  theme_bw()+
  theme(legend.position = "none")+
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(100, 200))
## Some of the focal terms are of type `character`. This may lead to
##   unexpected results. It is recommended to convert these variables to
##   factors before fitting the model.
##   The following variables are of type character: `Sex`
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.
pB2

#ggsave("B SBP and sex and age plot_mod1.png", plot= #last_plot(), bg = "white", height =10, width = 13, #units="cm" )

pC2<-plot_model(mod_healthy, type = "emm", color = "black", terms = c("Age_years[all]")) +
  xlab("Age (years)") +
  ylab("SBP (mmHg)") +
  ggtitle("A") +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(100, 200))
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.
#ggsave("C SBP and age plot_healthy cats only_mod4.png", #plot= last_plot(), bg = "white", height =10, width = #10, #units="cm" )

pD2<-plot_model(mod_healthy, type="emm",color=c("#00BFC4", "#F8766D"), terms=c("Age_years[all]", "Sex"))+ 
  xlab("Age (years)")+
  ylab("SBP (mmHg)") + 
  ggtitle("B")+  
  theme_bw()+
  theme(legend.position="none")+
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(100, 200))
## Some of the focal terms are of type `character`. This may lead to
##   unexpected results. It is recommended to convert these variables to
##   factors before fitting the model.
##   The following variables are of type character: `Sex`
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.
#ggsave("D SBP and sex and age plot_healthy cats #only_mod4.png", plot= last_plot(), bg = "white", height #=10, width = 13, units="cm" )




#patchwork together 

plotsA2_B2<-(pA2 | pB2) 

#ggsave("plotsA2_B2.png", plot=plotsA2_B2, bg = "white", #height =10, width = 24, units="cm" )

plots_healthy2_bw<-(pC2 | pD2)


#ggsave("plots_healthy2_bw.png", plot=plots_healthy2_bw,
       
plot_model(mod_int,
           type = "emm",
           color = c("orange", "purple"),
           terms = c("Age_years[all]", "Overall_health_status")) +
  xlab("Age (years)") +
  ylab("SBP (mmHg)") +
  ggtitle("") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(100, 200))
## Some of the focal terms are of type `character`. This may lead to
##   unexpected results. It is recommended to convert these variables to
##   factors before fitting the model.
##   The following variables are of type character: `Overall_health_status`
## Scale for y is already present.
## Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

#ggsave("Fig.3.SBP and sex and health plot.png", plot= #last_plot(), bg = "white", height =10, width = 13, #units="cm" )

##vii. emmeans

library(emmeans)
## Welcome to emmeans.
## Caution: You lose important information if you filter this package's results.
## See '? untidy'
## 
## Attaching package: 'emmeans'
## The following object is masked from 'package:rms':
## 
##     contrast
em <- emmeans(mod_b2_nested, ~ Age_years,
              at = list(Age_years = c(6,7,8,9, 10, 10.5, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16)),
              re.form = NA)  # Omit random effects (population-level prediction)
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
summary(em)
##  Age_years emmean   SE  df lower.CL upper.CL
##        6.0    118 2.16 761      114      122
##        7.0    122 1.63 450      118      125
##        8.0    125 1.25 202      123      128
##        9.0    129 1.09 132      127      131
##       10.0    133 1.12 148      131      135
##       10.5    135 1.15 162      133      137
##       11.0    137 1.17 169      135      140
##       12.0    142 1.19 176      140      144
##       13.0    147 1.29 228      145      150
##       14.0    153 1.57 425      150      156
##       15.0    158 2.04 763      154      162
##       16.0    164 2.65 995      159      169
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: Sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger 
## Confidence level used: 0.95
em_sex <- emmeans(mod_b2_nested, ~ Age_years + Sex,
              at = list(Age_years = c(6,7,8,9, 10, 10.5, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17)),
              re.form = NA)  # Omit random effects (population-level prediction)

summary(em_sex)
##  Age_years Sex    emmean   SE   df lower.CL upper.CL
##        6.0 Female    113 2.95  984      107      119
##        7.0 Female    118 2.21  692      113      122
##        8.0 Female    122 1.66  333      119      125
##        9.0 Female    126 1.46  213      123      129
##       10.0 Female    130 1.52  237      127      133
##       10.5 Female    132 1.57  255      129      136
##       11.0 Female    134 1.60  264      131      137
##       12.0 Female    138 1.63  266      135      141
##       13.0 Female    141 1.77  328      138      145
##       14.0 Female    145 2.15  561      140      149
##       15.0 Female    148 2.80  893      142      153
##       16.0 Female    151 3.64 1064      144      158
##       17.0 Female    154 4.57 1099      145      163
##        6.0 Male      123 3.09  977      117      129
##        7.0 Male      126 2.33  676      121      130
##        8.0 Male      128 1.76  319      125      132
##        9.0 Male      131 1.54  193      128      134
##       10.0 Male      135 1.57  203      132      138
##       10.5 Male      138 1.61  216      134      141
##       11.0 Male      140 1.63  222      137      143
##       12.0 Male      146 1.67  228      143      150
##       13.0 Male      153 1.82  299      150      157
##       14.0 Male      161 2.24  549      156      165
##       15.0 Male      169 2.93  895      163      174
##       16.0 Male      177 3.82 1061      169      184
##       17.0 Male      185 4.79 1092      176      195
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger 
## Confidence level used: 0.95
em_healthy <- emmeans(mod_healthy, ~ Age_years,
              at = list(Age_years = c(6,7,8,9, 10, 10.5, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17)),
              re.form = NA)  # Omit random effects (population-level prediction)
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
summary(em_healthy )
##  Age_years emmean    SE  df lower.CL upper.CL
##        6.0    118 2.470 626      113      123
##        7.0    121 1.700 462      118      125
##        8.0    124 1.140 195      122      127
##        9.0    127 0.972 124      126      129
##       10.0    131 1.040 157      129      133
##       10.5    132 1.060 165      130      134
##       11.0    134 1.070 168      131      136
##       12.0    137 1.210 234      134      139
##       13.0    139 1.690 473      136      143
##       14.0    142 2.480 656      137      147
##       15.0    145 3.460 696      138      152
##       16.0    148 4.510 700      139      157
##       17.0    151 5.590 697      140      162
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: Sex 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger 
## Confidence level used: 0.95
em_healthy_sex <- emmeans(mod_healthy, ~ Age_years + Sex,
              at = list(Age_years = c(6,7,8,9, 10, 10.5, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17)),
              re.form = NA)  # Omit random effects (population-level prediction)

summary(em_healthy_sex)
##  Age_years Sex    emmean   SE  df lower.CL upper.CL
##        6.0 Female    113 3.29 671      107      119
##        7.0 Female    118 2.29 561      113      122
##        8.0 Female    122 1.53 290      119      125
##        9.0 Female    126 1.30 185      123      129
##       10.0 Female    129 1.40 226      127      132
##       10.5 Female    131 1.44 236      128      133
##       11.0 Female    132 1.46 232      129      135
##       12.0 Female    134 1.55 243      130      137
##       13.0 Female    135 1.93 384      131      139
##       14.0 Female    135 2.70 592      130      141
##       15.0 Female    136 3.72 682      129      143
##       16.0 Female    136 4.85 700      127      146
##       17.0 Female    137 6.02 700      125      149
##        6.0 Male      123 3.64 689      116      130
##        7.0 Male      125 2.48 595      120      130
##        8.0 Male      127 1.63 299      124      130
##        9.0 Male      129 1.40 185      126      132
##       10.0 Male      132 1.50 211      129      135
##       10.5 Male      133 1.52 209      130      136
##       11.0 Male      135 1.54 207      132      138
##       12.0 Male      140 1.85 313      136      143
##       13.0 Male      144 2.76 582      139      150
##       14.0 Male      149 4.16 688      141      157
##       15.0 Male      155 5.83 700      143      166
##       16.0 Male      160 7.61 698      145      175
##       17.0 Male      165 9.41 695      147      184

## Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger 
## Confidence level used: 0.95

##viii. sample sizes per model

tbl_regression(mod_bc_nested, exp=T)%>%add_n(location="level")

#for cat numbers?
#check N per disease in this model
Metadata_mod_bc_nested<- Metadata_SBP2 %>%
  select(Cat_ID, Cat_ID_timepoint, Sex, BCS_group3, CKD, Hyperthyroidism, 
         Hypertension, Neoplasia, HM_group, Diabetes_mellitus, 
         Dental_disease, HR, Breed, MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe)
#remove NAs
Metadata_mod_bc_nested<-na.omit(Metadata_mod_bc_nested)

distinct_cats_per_covariate_mod_bc_nested <- Metadata_mod_bc_nested %>%
  summarise(
    BCS5=n_distinct(Cat_ID[BCS_group3== "5"]),
     BCS14=n_distinct(Cat_ID[BCS_group3== "1-4"]),
     BCS6=n_distinct(Cat_ID[BCS_group3== "6"]),
     BCS79=n_distinct(Cat_ID[BCS_group3== "7-9"]),
     MCS3=n_distinct(Cat_ID[MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe== "None"]),
    MCS2=n_distinct(Cat_ID[MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe== "Mild"]),
    MCS01=n_distinct(Cat_ID[MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe== "Moderate to severe"]),
    CKD = n_distinct(Cat_ID[CKD == "Yes"]),
    Hyperthyroidism = n_distinct(Cat_ID[Hyperthyroidism == "Yes"]),
    Hypertension = n_distinct(Cat_ID[Hypertension == "Yes"]),
    Neoplasia = n_distinct(Cat_ID[Neoplasia == "Yes"]),
    HM_group0 = n_distinct(Cat_ID[HM_group == "None"]),
    HM_group12 = n_distinct(Cat_ID[HM_group  == "I-II/VI"]),
    HM_group3 = n_distinct(Cat_ID[HM_group  == "≥III/VI"]),
    Diabetes_mellitus = n_distinct(Cat_ID[Diabetes_mellitus == "Yes"]),
    Dental_disease = n_distinct(Cat_ID[Dental_disease == "Yes"]),
     Sex = n_distinct(Cat_ID[Sex == "Male"]),
    Breed_ped=n_distinct(Cat_ID[Breed =="Pedigree"])
  )

#Print the number of distinct cats that meet the conditions
print(distinct_cats_per_covariate_mod_bc_nested)
##   BCS5 BCS14 BCS6 BCS79 MCS3 MCS2 MCS01 CKD Hyperthyroidism Hypertension
## 1  102    63   98   102  167  152    37  22              18           51
##   Neoplasia HM_group0 HM_group12 HM_group3 Diabetes_mellitus Dental_disease Sex
## 1         7       176        113        26                 4            166  95
##   Breed_ped
## 1        28

####and for mod_oe

#for cat numbers?
#check N per disease in this model
Metadata_mod_oe_nested<- Metadata_SBP2 %>%
  select(Cat_ID, Cat_ID_timepoint, Sex, BCS_group3, CKD, Hyperthyroidism, 
         Hypertension, Neoplasia, HM_group, Diabetes_mellitus, 
         Dental_disease, HR, Breed, MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe, oe.abnormality_score)
#remove NAs
Metadata_mod_oe_nested<-na.omit(Metadata_mod_oe_nested)

distinct_cats_per_covariate_mod_oe_nested <- Metadata_mod_oe_nested %>%
  summarise(
    BCS5=n_distinct(Cat_ID[BCS_group3== "5"]),
     BCS14=n_distinct(Cat_ID[BCS_group3== "1-4"]),
     BCS6=n_distinct(Cat_ID[BCS_group3== "6"]),
     BCS79=n_distinct(Cat_ID[BCS_group3== "7-9"]),
     MCS3=n_distinct(Cat_ID[MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe== "None"]),
    MCS2=n_distinct(Cat_ID[MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe== "Mild"]),
    MCS01=n_distinct(Cat_ID[MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe== "Moderate to severe"]),
    CKD = n_distinct(Cat_ID[CKD == "Yes"]),
    Hyperthyroidism = n_distinct(Cat_ID[Hyperthyroidism == "Yes"]),
    Hypertension = n_distinct(Cat_ID[Hypertension == "Yes"]),
    Neoplasia = n_distinct(Cat_ID[Neoplasia == "Yes"]),
    HM_group0 = n_distinct(Cat_ID[HM_group == "None"]),
    HM_group12 = n_distinct(Cat_ID[HM_group  == "I-II/VI"]),
    HM_group3 = n_distinct(Cat_ID[HM_group  == "≥III/VI"]),
    Diabetes_mellitus = n_distinct(Cat_ID[Diabetes_mellitus == "Yes"]),
    Dental_disease = n_distinct(Cat_ID[Dental_disease == "Yes"]),
     Sex = n_distinct(Cat_ID[Sex == "Male"]),
    Breed_ped=n_distinct(Cat_ID[Breed =="Pedigree"])
  )

#Print the number of distinct cats that meet the conditions
print(distinct_cats_per_covariate_mod_oe_nested)
##   BCS5 BCS14 BCS6 BCS79 MCS3 MCS2 MCS01 CKD Hyperthyroidism Hypertension
## 1   72    52   71    73  120  122    30  19              16           38
##   Neoplasia HM_group0 HM_group12 HM_group3 Diabetes_mellitus Dental_disease Sex
## 1         4       137         92        23                 3            130  85
##   Breed_ped
## 1        26

#####and for healthy

#for cat numbers?
#check N per disease in this model
Metadata_mod_healthy<- Metadata_SBP2 %>%
  filter(Healthy_visit=="Yes")%>%
  select(Cat_ID, Cat_ID_timepoint, Sex, Age_years)
#remove NAs
Metadata_mod_healthy<-na.omit(Metadata_mod_healthy)

distinct_cats_per_covariate_mod_healthy<- Metadata_mod_healthy %>%
  summarise(
     SexM = n_distinct(Cat_ID[Sex == "Male"]),
     SexF = n_distinct(Cat_ID[Sex == "Female"]),
  )

#Print the number of distinct cats that meet the conditions
print(distinct_cats_per_covariate_mod_healthy)
##   SexM SexF
## 1   79   91
dim(Metadata_mod_healthy)
## [1] 707   4

#7. model visits where SBP >=160mmHg ##a. binomial model of hypertension

Metadata_SBP2$Hypertension<-as.factor(Metadata_SBP2$Hypertension)

#full model

mod_ht<-glmer(Hypertension ~ Age_years + Sex + Breed + BCS_group3 + MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe + CKD + Hyperthyroidism + Neoplasia + Diabetes_mellitus + HM_group + Dental_disease + (1|Cat_ID), data= Metadata_SBP2, family=binomial)
## Warning in (function (fn, par, lower = rep.int(-Inf, n), upper = rep.int(Inf, :
## failure to converge in 10000 evaluations
## Warning in optwrap(optimizer, devfun, start, rho$lower, control = control, :
## convergence code 4 from Nelder_Mead: failure to converge in 10000 evaluations
## Warning in checkConv(attr(opt, "derivs"), opt$par, ctrl = control$checkConv, :
## unable to evaluate scaled gradient
## Warning in checkConv(attr(opt, "derivs"), opt$par, ctrl = control$checkConv, :
## Hessian is numerically singular: parameters are not uniquely determined
#
summary(mod_ht)
## Warning in vcov.merMod(object, use.hessian = use.hessian): variance-covariance matrix computed from finite-difference Hessian is
## not positive definite or contains NA values: falling back to var-cov estimated from RX
## Warning in vcov.merMod(object, correlation = correlation, sigm = sig): variance-covariance matrix computed from finite-difference Hessian is
## not positive definite or contains NA values: falling back to var-cov estimated from RX
## Generalized linear mixed model fit by maximum likelihood (Laplace
##   Approximation) [glmerMod]
##  Family: binomial  ( logit )
## Formula: 
## Hypertension ~ Age_years + Sex + Breed + BCS_group3 + MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe +  
##     CKD + Hyperthyroidism + Neoplasia + Diabetes_mellitus + HM_group +  
##     Dental_disease + (1 | Cat_ID)
##    Data: Metadata_SBP2
## 
##       AIC       BIC    logLik -2*log(L)  df.resid 
##     506.1     590.0    -236.0     472.1      1010 
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##    Min     1Q Median     3Q    Max 
## -3.085 -0.129 -0.036 -0.011 64.859 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  Cat_ID (Intercept) 13.16    3.628   
## Number of obs: 1027, groups:  Cat_ID, 200
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                                                            Estimate Std. Error
## (Intercept)                                              -1.639e+01  1.700e+00
## Age_years                                                 8.132e-01  1.286e-01
## SexMale                                                   2.349e+00  9.079e-01
## BreedPedigree                                            -5.787e-01  1.287e+00
## BCS_group31-4                                             1.112e+00  7.307e-01
## BCS_group36                                              -8.250e-01  6.376e-01
## BCS_group37-9                                             7.457e-01  6.442e-01
## MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_SevereMild                1.029e+00  4.810e-01
## MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_SevereModerate to severe  1.133e+00  8.801e-01
## CKDYes                                                    1.047e+00  8.966e-01
## HyperthyroidismYes                                        2.239e+00  1.015e+00
## NeoplasiaYes                                             -9.846e+01  2.373e+07
## Diabetes_mellitusYes                                      1.371e+00  2.333e+00
## HM_groupI-II/VI                                           4.619e-01  4.397e-01
## HM_group≥III/VI                                          -1.147e+00  1.039e+00
## Dental_diseaseYes                                         5.083e-01  3.814e-01
##                                                          z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)                                               -9.645  < 2e-16 ***
## Age_years                                                  6.322 2.58e-10 ***
## SexMale                                                    2.587  0.00968 ** 
## BreedPedigree                                             -0.450  0.65294    
## BCS_group31-4                                              1.522  0.12799    
## BCS_group36                                               -1.294  0.19571    
## BCS_group37-9                                              1.158  0.24704    
## MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_SevereMild                 2.140  0.03239 *  
## MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_SevereModerate to severe   1.287  0.19805    
## CKDYes                                                     1.167  0.24307

####model fails to converge? neoplasia infinite

#remove neoplasia and diabetic cases as too few samples
Metadata_SBP_nodmorneo<- Metadata_SBP2 %>%
  filter(Diabetes_mellitus != "Yes")

Metadata_SBP_nodmorneo<- Metadata_SBP_nodmorneo %>%
  filter(Neoplasia != "Yes")

#re-run model

mod_ht<-glmer(Hypertension ~ Age_years + Sex + Breed + HR + BCS_group3 + MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe + CKD + Hyperthyroidism + HM_group + Dental_disease + (1|Cat_ID), data= Metadata_SBP_nodmorneo, family=binomial) #failed to converge
## Warning in checkConv(attr(opt, "derivs"), opt$par, ctrl = control$checkConv, :
## Model failed to converge with max|grad| = 2.30333 (tol = 0.002, component 1)
## Warning in checkConv(attr(opt, "derivs"), opt$par, ctrl = control$checkConv, : Model is nearly unidentifiable: large eigenvalue ratio
##  - Rescale variables?
mod_ht<-glmer(Hypertension ~ Age_years + Sex + BCS_group3 + MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe + CKD + Hyperthyroidism + Heart_murmur+ Dental_disease + (1|Cat_ID), data= Metadata_SBP_nodmorneo, family=binomial) #failed to converge
## Warning in checkConv(attr(opt, "derivs"), opt$par, ctrl = control$checkConv, :
## Model failed to converge with max|grad| = 1.04063 (tol = 0.002, component 1)
#
summary(mod_ht)

mod_ht<-glmer(Hypertension ~ Age_years + Sex + Breed + HR + BCS_group3 + MCS_grade_None_Mild_Moderate_to_Severe + CKD + Hyperthyroidism + HM_group + Dental_disease + (1|Cat_ID), data= Metadata_SBP_nodmorneo, family=binomial) #failed to converge

##b. GEE models for SBP >160 appointments ###i. sort data

#change MCS to binary Yes/No to reduce variables
Metadata_SBP2 <- Metadata_SBP2 %>%
  mutate(MCS_binary = case_when(
    MCS_combined == 30 ~ "No",
    MCS_combined %in% 0:29 ~ "Yes",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  ))
#create dataframe without NAs

Metadata_SBP_gee<-na.omit(Metadata_SBP2[  ,c("Hypertension", "Cat_ID", "Owner_ID", "Age_years", "Sex", "Breed", "BCS_group3", "MCS_binary", "HR", "CKD", "Diabetes_mellitus", "Neoplasia", "SBP", "Hyperthyroidism", "Heart_murmur", "Dental_disease")])

#create numbered hypertension column
dput(unique(Metadata_SBP_gee$Hypertension))
## structure(1:2, levels = c("No", "Yes"), class = "factor")
sum(Metadata_SBP_gee$Hypertension == "Yes")
## [1] 128
sum(is.na(Metadata_SBP_gee$Hypertension))
## [1] 0
Metadata_SBP_gee$Hypertension_num <-
  ifelse(Metadata_SBP_gee$Hypertension == "Yes", 1, 0)

sum(Metadata_SBP_gee$Hypertension_num)
## [1] 128
length(unique(Metadata_SBP_gee$Cat_ID[Metadata_SBP_gee$Hypertension_num=="1"]))
## [1] 51

###ii. try GEE package

#gee_ht<-gee(Hypertension_num ~ Sex + Breed, 
#            data=Metadata_SBP_gee,
#            id= Cat_ID,
#            family=binomial, 
#            corstr="exhangeable")
#won't work as some cats only have 1 measurement. Try geeglm

###iii. try geepack glm model

Metadata_SBP_gee$Cat_ID <- factor(Metadata_SBP_gee$Cat_ID)

gee_ht <- geeglm(
  Hypertension_num ~ Age_years + Sex + Breed + HR + BCS_group3 + MCS_binary + Diabetes_mellitus +  CKD + Hyperthyroidism +  Heart_murmur + Dental_disease,
  data = Metadata_SBP_gee,
  id = Cat_ID,
  family = binomial,
  corstr = "exchangeable"
)

tbl_regression(gee_ht, exp=TRUE, pvalue_fun = label_style_pvalue(digits = 3) )%>%add_n(location="level")
Characteristic N OR 95% CI p-value
Age_years 1,023 1.50 1.28, 1.75 <0.001
Sex



    Female 516 — —
    Male 507 3.39 1.46, 7.86 0.004
Breed



    Non-pedigree 879 — —
    Pedigree 144 0.99 0.38, 2.60 0.982
HR 1,023 1.01 1.00, 1.02 0.099
BCS_group3



    5 290 — —
    1-4 128 1.24 0.65, 2.36 0.517
    6 244 1.06 0.55, 2.03 0.873
    7-9 361 2.53 1.19, 5.37 0.016
MCS_binary



    No 491 — —
    Yes 532 2.36 1.33, 4.18 0.003
Diabetes_mellitus



    No 1,016 — —
    Yes 7 0.60 0.08, 4.60 0.626
CKD



    No 935 — —

Hyperthyroidism

Dental_disease



    No 437 — —

tab_model(gee_ht)
  Hypertension num
Predictors Odds Ratios CI p
(Intercept) 0.00 0.00 – 0.00 <0.001

HR 1.01 1.00 – 1.02 0.099
BCS_group31-4 1.24 0.65 – 2.36 0.517
BCS group3 [6] 1.06 0.55 – 2.03 0.873
BCS_group37-9 2.53 1.19 – 5.37 0.016
