summary(gee_ht)
## 
## Call:
## geeglm(formula = Hypertension_num ~ Age_years + Sex + Breed + 
##     HR + BCS_group3 + MCS_binary + Diabetes_mellitus + CKD + 
##     Hyperthyroidism + Heart_murmur + Dental_disease, family = binomial, 
##     data = Metadata_SBP_gee, id = Cat_ID, corstr = "exchangeable")
## 
##  Coefficients:
##                        Estimate    Std.err   Wald Pr(>|W|)    
## (Intercept)          -10.021433   1.346431 55.398 9.85e-14 ***
## Age_years              0.402798   0.080132 25.267 4.99e-07 ***
## SexMale                1.221720   0.428788  8.118  0.00438 ** 
## BreedPedigree         -0.011127   0.493734  0.001  0.98202    
## HR                     0.008298   0.005024  2.728  0.09857 .  
## BCS_group31-4          0.213835   0.329923  0.420  0.51690    
## BCS_group36            0.053679   0.334604  0.026  0.87255    
## BCS_group37-9          0.928486   0.384119  5.843  0.01564 *  
## MCS_binaryYes          0.857634   0.292418  8.602  0.00336 ** 
## Diabetes_mellitusYes  -0.505505   1.036946  0.238  0.62591    
## CKDYes                 0.458713   0.452518  1.028  0.31073    
## HyperthyroidismYes     1.176674   0.427626  7.572  0.00593 ** 
## Heart_murmurYes        0.118185   0.251671  0.221  0.63864    
## Dental_diseaseYes      0.150970   0.245144  0.379  0.53800    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation structure = exchangeable 
## Estimated Scale Parameters:
## 
##             Estimate Std.err
## (Intercept)    1.237   3.273
##   Link = identity 
## 
## Estimated Correlation Parameters:
##       Estimate Std.err
## alpha   0.1974  0.5226
## Number of clusters:   199  Maximum cluster size: 12

####check mod assumptions

check_collinearity(gee_ht)
## # Check for Multicollinearity
## 
## Low Correlation
## 
##               Term  VIF   VIF 95% CI adj. VIF Tolerance Tolerance 95% CI
##          Age_years 1.57 [1.46, 1.72]     1.25      0.64     [0.58, 0.69]
##                Sex 1.29 [1.21, 1.40]     1.13      0.78     [0.71, 0.83]
##              Breed 1.10 [1.05, 1.21]     1.05      0.91     [0.83, 0.95]
##                 HR 1.17 [1.11, 1.27]     1.08      0.85     [0.78, 0.90]
##         BCS_group3 1.77 [1.63, 1.94]     1.33      0.57     [0.52, 0.61]
##         MCS_binary 1.80 [1.66, 1.97]     1.34      0.56     [0.51, 0.60]
##  Diabetes_mellitus 1.13 [1.07, 1.23]     1.06      0.88     [0.81, 0.93]
##                CKD 1.15 [1.09, 1.25]     1.07      0.87     [0.80, 0.92]
##    Hyperthyroidism 1.41 [1.31, 1.53]     1.19      0.71     [0.65, 0.76]
##       Heart_murmur 1.14 [1.08, 1.24]     1.07      0.88     [0.80, 0.92]
##     Dental_disease 1.31 [1.23, 1.43]     1.15      0.76     [0.70, 0.81]
check_residuals(gee_ht)
## Warning in checkModel(fittedModel): DHARMa: fittedModel not in class of
## supported models. Absolutely no guarantee that this will work!
## OK: Simulated residuals appear as uniformly distributed (p = 0.957).

####check neoplasia results

with(Metadata_SBP_gee, table(Neoplasia, Hypertension_num))
##          Hypertension_num
## Neoplasia   0   1
##       No  887 128
##       Yes   8   0
#Zero hypertensive outcomes among neoplasia-positive cats

####remove neoplasia Neoplasia was excluded from multivariable models due to quasi-complete separation (no hypertensive outcomes observed among exposed cats), resulting in unstable estimates. ####GEE 1 FINAL MODEL

gee_ht2 <- geeglm(
  Hypertension_num ~ Age_years + Sex + Breed + HR + BCS_group3 + MCS_binary + Diabetes_mellitus +
    CKD + Hyperthyroidism +  Heart_murmur + Dental_disease,
  data = Metadata_SBP_gee,
  id = Cat_ID,
  family = binomial,
  corstr = "exchangeable"
)

tbl_regression(gee_ht2, pvalue_fun = label_style_pvalue(digits = 3), exp=TRUE)%>%add_n(location="level")
Characteristic N OR 95% CI p-value
Age_years 1,023 1.50 1.28, 1.75 <0.001
Sex



    Female 516 — —
    Male 507 3.39 1.46, 7.86 0.004
Breed



    Non-pedigree 879 — —
    Pedigree 144 0.99 0.38, 2.60 0.982
HR 1,023 1.01 1.00, 1.02 0.099
BCS_group3



    5 290 — —
    1-4 128 1.24 0.65, 2.36 0.517
    6 244 1.06 0.55, 2.03 0.873
    7-9 361 2.53 1.19, 5.37 0.016
MCS_binary



    No 491 — —
    Yes 532 2.36 1.33, 4.18 0.003
Diabetes_mellitus



    No 1,016 — —
    Yes 7 0.60 0.08, 4.60 0.626
CKD



    No 935 — —
    Yes 88 1.58 0.65, 3.84 0.311
Hyperthyroidism



    No 964 — —
    Yes 59 3.24 1.40, 7.50 0.006
Heart_murmur



    No 718 — —
    Yes 305 1.13 0.69, 1.84 0.639
Dental_disease



    No 437 — —
    Yes 586 1.16 0.72, 1.88 0.538
Abbreviations: CI = Confidence Interval, OR = Odds Ratio 
summary(gee_ht2)
## 
## Call:
## geeglm(formula = Hypertension_num ~ Age_years + Sex + Breed + 
##     HR + BCS_group3 + MCS_binary + Diabetes_mellitus + CKD + 
##     Hyperthyroidism + Heart_murmur + Dental_disease, family = binomial, 
##     data = Metadata_SBP_gee, id = Cat_ID, corstr = "exchangeable")
## 
##  Coefficients:
##                       Estimate   Std.err  Wald Pr(>|W|)    
## (Intercept)          -10.02143   1.34643 55.40  9.8e-14 ***
## Age_years              0.40280   0.08013 25.27  5.0e-07 ***
## SexMale                1.22172   0.42879  8.12   0.0044 ** 
## BreedPedigree         -0.01113   0.49373  0.00   0.9820    
## HR                     0.00830   0.00502  2.73   0.0986 .  
## BCS_group31-4          0.21383   0.32992  0.42   0.5169    
## BCS_group36            0.05368   0.33460  0.03   0.8725    
## BCS_group37-9          0.92849   0.38412  5.84   0.0156 *  
## MCS_binaryYes          0.85763   0.29242  8.60   0.0034 ** 
## Diabetes_mellitusYes  -0.50550   1.03695  0.24   0.6259    
## CKDYes                 0.45871   0.45252  1.03   0.3107    
## HyperthyroidismYes     1.17667   0.42763  7.57   0.0059 ** 
## Heart_murmurYes        0.11818   0.25167  0.22   0.6386    
## Dental_diseaseYes      0.15097   0.24514  0.38   0.5380    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation structure = exchangeable 
## Estimated Scale Parameters:
## 
##             Estimate Std.err
## (Intercept)     1.24    3.27
##   Link = identity 
## 
## Estimated Correlation Parameters:
##       Estimate Std.err
## alpha    0.197   0.523
## Number of clusters:   199  Maximum cluster size: 12

####check over-parameterised

table(Metadata_SBP_gee$Hypertension_num)
## 
##   0   1 
## 895 128
# Count of events
length(coef(gee_ht2))
## [1] 14
# Number of estimated parameters
#EPV=7

summary(gee_ht2)$coefficients[, "Std.err"]
##  [1] 1.34643 0.08013 0.42879 0.49373 0.00502 0.32992 0.33460 0.38412 0.29242
## [10] 1.03695 0.45252 0.42763 0.25167 0.24514
#correlation

vif(lm(Hypertension_num ~ Age_years + Sex + Breed + HR + BCS_group3 + MCS_binary + Diabetes_mellitus +
    CKD + Hyperthyroidism +  Heart_murmur + Dental_disease, data = Metadata_SBP_gee))
##            Age_years              SexMale        BreedPedigree 
##                 1.45                 1.10                 1.06 
##                   HR        BCS_group31-4          BCS_group36 
##                 1.04                 1.34                 1.47 
##        BCS_group37-9        MCS_binaryYes Diabetes_mellitusYes 
##                 1.49                 1.49                 1.02 
##               CKDYes   HyperthyroidismYes      Heart_murmurYes 
##                 1.05                 1.08                 1.08 
##    Dental_diseaseYes 
##                 1.01
check_model(gee_ht2)
## Converting missing values (`NA`) into regular values currently not
##   possible for variables of class `NULL`.

check_collinearity(gee_ht2)
## # Check for Multicollinearity
## 
## Low Correlation
## 
##               Term  VIF   VIF 95% CI adj. VIF Tolerance Tolerance 95% CI
##          Age_years 1.57 [1.46, 1.72]     1.25      0.64     [0.58, 0.69]
##                Sex 1.29 [1.21, 1.40]     1.13      0.78     [0.71, 0.83]
##              Breed 1.10 [1.05, 1.21]     1.05      0.91     [0.83, 0.95]
##                 HR 1.17 [1.11, 1.27]     1.08      0.85     [0.78, 0.90]
##         BCS_group3 1.77 [1.63, 1.94]     1.33      0.57     [0.52, 0.61]
##         MCS_binary 1.80 [1.66, 1.97]     1.34      0.56     [0.51, 0.60]
##  Diabetes_mellitus 1.13 [1.07, 1.23]     1.06      0.88     [0.81, 0.93]
##                CKD 1.15 [1.09, 1.25]     1.07      0.87     [0.80, 0.92]
##    Hyperthyroidism 1.41 [1.31, 1.53]     1.19      0.71     [0.65, 0.76]
##       Heart_murmur 1.14 [1.08, 1.24]     1.07      0.88     [0.80, 0.92]
##     Dental_disease 1.31 [1.23, 1.43]     1.15      0.76     [0.70, 0.81]

##iii. GEE2 for OE score also

Metadata_SBP_gee_oe<-na.omit(Metadata_SBP2[ ,c("Hypertension", "Cat_ID", "Owner_ID", "Age_years", "Sex", "Breed", "BCS_group3", "MCS_binary", "HR", "CKD", "SBP", "Diabetes_mellitus", "Hyperthyroidism", "Heart_murmur", "HR", "Dental_disease", "oe.abnormality_score", "Healthy_visit")])

#create numbered column 
Metadata_SBP_gee_oe$Hypertension_num <-
  ifelse(Metadata_SBP_gee_oe$Hypertension == "Yes", 1, 0)

sum(Metadata_SBP_gee_oe$Hypertension_num)
## [1] 77
length(unique(Metadata_SBP_gee_oe$Cat_ID[Metadata_SBP_gee_oe$Hypertension_num=="1"]))
## [1] 38
#change Cat_ID to factor

Metadata_SBP_gee_oe$Cat_ID <- factor(Metadata_SBP_gee_oe$Cat_ID)
#run model

gee_ht_oe <- geeglm(
  Hypertension_num ~ Age_years + Sex + Breed + HR + BCS_group3 + MCS_binary + 
    CKD + Hyperthyroidism  + Heart_murmur + Diabetes_mellitus  + Dental_disease +oe.abnormality_score,
  data = Metadata_SBP_gee_oe,
  id = Cat_ID,
  family = binomial,
  corstr = "exchangeable"
)

#tab model

tbl_regression(gee_ht_oe,  pvalue_fun = label_style_pvalue(digits = 3), exp=TRUE)%>%add_n(location="level")
Characteristic N OR 95% CI p-value
Age_years 569 1.33 1.05, 1.68 0.018
Sex



    Female 265 — —
    Male 304 5.50 1.87, 16.2 0.002
Breed



    Non-pedigree 487 — —
    Pedigree 82 0.90 0.30, 2.65 0.848
HR 569 1.01 1.00, 1.02 0.071
BCS_group3



    5 165 — —
    1-4 83 2.42 0.80, 7.27 0.116
    6 140 1.44 0.54, 3.86 0.464
    7-9 181 3.71 1.39, 9.89 0.009
MCS_binary



    No 227 — —
    Yes 342 1.85 0.76, 4.51 0.175
CKD



    No 504 — —
    Yes 65 1.90 0.72, 5.01 0.193
Hyperthyroidism



    No 532 — —
    Yes 37 6.64 2.49, 17.7 <0.001
Heart_murmur



    No 369 — —
    Yes 200 1.15 0.59, 2.25 0.675
Diabetes_mellitus



    No 563 — —

Dental_disease



    No 230 — —
    Yes 339 0.79 0.43, 1.47 0.464
oe.abnormality_score 569 1.10 1.01, 1.19 0.022

tab_model(gee_ht_oe)

Predictors Odds Ratios CI p
(Intercept) 0.00 0.00 – 0.00 <0.001

Observations 569

#check model

check_collinearity(gee_ht_oe)
## # Check for Multicollinearity
## 
## Low Correlation
## 
##                  Term  VIF   VIF 95% CI adj. VIF Tolerance Tolerance 95% CI
##             Age_years 2.53 [2.24, 2.89]     1.59      0.40     [0.35, 0.45]
##                   Sex 1.79 [1.61, 2.02]     1.34      0.56     [0.49, 0.62]
##                 Breed 1.24 [1.14, 1.39]     1.11      0.81     [0.72, 0.87]
##                    HR 1.45 [1.32, 1.63]     1.21      0.69     [0.61, 0.76]
##            BCS_group3 2.96 [2.61, 3.39]     1.72      0.34     [0.29, 0.38]
##            MCS_binary 1.96 [1.75, 2.22]     1.40      0.51     [0.45, 0.57]
##                   CKD 1.32 [1.21, 1.48]     1.15      0.76     [0.68, 0.83]
##       Hyperthyroidism 1.76 [1.59, 1.99]     1.33      0.57     [0.50, 0.63]
##          Heart_murmur 1.41 [1.29, 1.58]     1.19      0.71     [0.63, 0.78]
##     Diabetes_mellitus 1.18 [1.10, 1.33]     1.09      0.84     [0.75, 0.91]
##        Dental_disease 1.60 [1.44, 1.80]     1.26      0.63     [0.56, 0.69]
##  oe.abnormality_score 2.20 [1.96, 2.51]     1.48      0.45     [0.40, 0.51]
check_model(gee_ht_oe)
## Converting missing values (`NA`) into regular values currently not
##   possible for variables of class `NULL`.

#8. TTE - Cox PH model

##a. sort data ###i. create baseline dataset

Metadata_hyp_baseline <- Metadata_SBP2 %>%
  arrange(Cat_ID, date) %>%
  group_by(Cat_ID) %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  ungroup()

###ii. Merge new incidence_data looking at censoring at age at last study visit NOT last study date

Hypertension_incidence_data2170326 <- read.csv("Hypertension_incidence_data2170326.csv")

Metadata_hyp_baseline2 <- Hypertension_incidence_data2170326 %>%
  left_join(
    Metadata_SBP2 %>%
      select(
        Cat_ID_timepoint,
        BCS_group3,
        Heart_murmur,
        HR,
        CKD,
        Hyperthyroidism,
        Dental_disease,
        oe.abnormalities_Yes_no_incomplete, 
        MCS_binary, 
        SBP
      ),
    by = "Cat_ID_timepoint"
  )

nrow(Metadata_hyp_baseline2)
## [1] 203
colnames(Metadata_hyp_baseline2)
##  [1] "Cat_ID_timepoint"                   "Cat_ID"                            
##  [3] "Owner_ID"                           "date"                              
##  [5] "dob"                                "Age_years"                         
##  [7] "Breed"                              "Sex"                               
##  [9] "BCS"                                "MCS_combined"                      
## [11] "hypertensive_sbp"                   "Hypertension"                      
## [13] "hypertensive_over.study"            "stHyp"                             
## [15] "X1st_visit_date"                    "X1st_visit_age"                    
## [17] "Censor_visit_date"                  "Censor_visit_age"                  
## [19] "baseline_dx"                        "BCS_group3"                        
## [21] "Heart_murmur"                       "HR"                                
## [23] "CKD"                                "Hyperthyroidism"                   
## [25] "Dental_disease"                     "oe.abnormalities_Yes_no_incomplete"
## [27] "MCS_binary"                         "SBP"
#no BCS <3 at baseline -change name of BCS_group to account for this
Metadata_hyp_baseline2 <- 
Metadata_hyp_baseline2 %>%
  mutate(BCS_group3 = case_when(
    BCS  %in% 3:4 ~ "3-4",
    BCS  == 5 ~ "5",
    BCS  == 6 ~ "6",
    BCS %in% 7:9 ~ "7-9",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  ))
Metadata_hyp_baseline2$BCS_group3
##   [1] "7-9" "7-9" "6"   "6"   "7-9" "3-4" "7-9" "6"   "5"   "7-9" "5"   "3-4"
##  [13] "5"   "7-9" "6"   "6"   "3-4" "3-4" "3-4" "5"   "7-9" "3-4" "5"   "6"  
##  [25] "3-4" "6"   "5"   "7-9" "7-9" "6"   "3-4" "3-4" "7-9" "7-9" "3-4" "6"  
##  [37] "6"   "3-4" "3-4" "5"   "5"   "5"   "3-4" "5"   "7-9" "6"   "5"   "5"  
##  [49] "7-9" "5"   "3-4" "6"   "6"   "6"   "5"   "3-4" "7-9" "3-4" "6"   "7-9"
##  [61] "5"   "6"   "6"   "7-9" "5"   "3-4" "6"   "7-9" "6"   "5"   "3-4" "6"  
##  [73] "7-9" "7-9" "7-9" "7-9" "5"   "7-9" "6"   "5"   "7-9" "5"   "3-4" "5"  
##  [85] "6"   "6"   "6"   "7-9" "7-9" "6"   "5"   "5"   "5"   "3-4" "3-4" "7-9"
##  [97] "3-4" "6"   "3-4" "7-9" "3-4" "5"   "6"   "7-9" "7-9" "5"   "7-9" "6"  
## [109] "5"   "5"   "6"   "3-4" "5"   "5"   "6"   "5"   "7-9" "6"   "7-9" "7-9"
## [121] "5"   "7-9" "5"   "6"   "6"   "7-9" "6"   "5"   "3-4" "5"   "6"   "6"  
## [133] "7-9" "7-9" "5"   "6"   "7-9" "7-9" "5"   "6"   "3-4" "7-9" "7-9" "7-9"
## [145] "7-9" "6"   "5"   "5"   "7-9" "6"   "6"   "7-9" "6"   "7-9" "7-9" "7-9"
## [157] "7-9" "3-4" "7-9" "6"   "5"   "7-9" "7-9" "7-9" "6"   "5"   "7-9" "5"  
## [169] "3-4" "5"   "7-9" "6"   "5"   "6"   "6"   "6"   "5"   "5"   "7-9" "7-9"
## [181] "5"   "3-4" "5"   "7-9" "7-9" "7-9" "5"   "5"   "7-9" "7-9" "5"   "6"  
## [193] "6"   "3-4" "3-4" "5"   "5"   "5"   "7-9" "7-9" "7-9" "6"   "6"
#set ref bcs category as 5 
Metadata_hyp_baseline2  <- Metadata_hyp_baseline2 %>%
  mutate(BCS_group3 = factor(
    BCS_group3,
    levels = c("5", "3-4", "6", "7-9")
  ))

####iii. remove those cats diagnosed at baseline

Metadata_hyp_baseline2<-Metadata_hyp_baseline2%>%
  filter(baseline_dx =="No")

##b. Check MCS missingness

#there are 11 missing MCS at baseline. does MCS affect hypertension risk?

cox_mcs<-coxph(Surv(Censor_visit_age, stHyp ) ~ MCS_combined,
                    data = Metadata_hyp_baseline2)

summary(cox_mcs)#not significant
## Call:
## coxph(formula = Surv(Censor_visit_age, stHyp) ~ MCS_combined, 
##     data = Metadata_hyp_baseline2)
## 
##   n= 185, number of events= 31 
##    (11 observations deleted due to missingness)
## 
##                 coef exp(coef) se(coef)     z Pr(>|z|)
## MCS_combined -0.0505    0.9508   0.0887 -0.57     0.57
## 
##              exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
## MCS_combined     0.951       1.05     0.799      1.13
## 
## Concordance= 0.511  (se = 0.046 )
## Likelihood ratio test= 0.3  on 1 df,   p=0.6
## Wald test            = 0.32  on 1 df,   p=0.6
## Score (logrank) test = 0.33  on 1 df,   p=0.6
tab_model(cox_mcs)
  Surv(Censor visit age,st
Hyp)
Predictors Estimates CI p
MCS combined 0.95 0.80 – 1.13 0.569
Observations 185
R2 Nagelkerke 0.002 
#and binary?

cox_mcs<-coxph(Surv(Censor_visit_age, stHyp ) ~ MCS_binary,
                    data = Metadata_hyp_baseline2)

summary(cox_mcs)#not significant
## Call:
## coxph(formula = Surv(Censor_visit_age, stHyp) ~ MCS_binary, data = Metadata_hyp_baseline2)
## 
##   n= 185, number of events= 31 
##    (11 observations deleted due to missingness)
## 
##                coef exp(coef) se(coef)    z Pr(>|z|)
## MCS_binaryYes 0.516     1.676    0.410 1.26     0.21
## 
##               exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
## MCS_binaryYes      1.68      0.597      0.75      3.75
## 
## Concordance= 0.512  (se = 0.044 )
## Likelihood ratio test= 1.48  on 1 df,   p=0.2
## Wald test            = 1.58  on 1 df,   p=0.2
## Score (logrank) test = 1.62  on 1 df,   p=0.2
tab_model(cox_mcs)
  Surv(Censor visit age,st
Hyp)
Predictors Estimates CI p
MCS binary [Yes] 1.68 0.75 – 3.75 0.208
Observations 185
R2 Nagelkerke 0.011

#####MCS not significant and missingness biases model - remove variable

##c. create complete case dataset

#complete cases

Metadata_hyp_baseline_for_cox<-na.omit(Metadata_hyp_baseline2[,c("Cat_ID", "Age_years", "Censor_visit_age", "stHyp", "Sex", "SBP", "Breed", "BCS_group3")])

dim(Metadata_hyp_baseline_for_cox)
## [1] 196   8
median(Metadata_hyp_baseline_for_cox$Age_years)
## [1] 8.13
quantile(Metadata_hyp_baseline_for_cox$Age_years, 0.25)
##  25% 
## 7.23
quantile(Metadata_hyp_baseline_for_cox$Age_years, 0.75)
##  75% 
## 9.25
min(Metadata_hyp_baseline_for_cox$Age_years)
## [1] 6.67
max(Metadata_hyp_baseline_for_cox$Age_years)
## [1] 11.2
Metadata_hyp_baseline_for_cox%>%
  filter(stHyp==2)
##    Cat_ID Age_years Censor_visit_age stHyp    Sex SBP        Breed BCS_group3
## 1  Cat006      7.52            10.13     2   Male 153     Pedigree        3-4
## 2  Cat007      7.03            11.18     2   Male 141     Pedigree        7-9
## 3  Cat011      7.11            11.87     2 Female 116 Non-pedigree          5
## 4  Cat026      9.19            13.21     2   Male 122 Non-pedigree          6
## 5  Cat028      9.56            14.66     2 Female 113 Non-pedigree        7-9
## 6  Cat029      9.56            14.66     2 Female 115 Non-pedigree        7-9
## 7  Cat034      9.00            13.51     2 Female 137 Non-pedigree        7-9
## 8  Cat038      9.82            13.07     2   Male 152 Non-pedigree        3-4
## 9  Cat042      9.93            14.38     2   Male 127 Non-pedigree          5
## 10 Cat045      8.36            12.30     2   Male 140 Non-pedigree        7-9
## 11 Cat048      9.10            12.30     2   Male 108 Non-pedigree          5
## 12 Cat056      9.50            14.14     2   Male 111 Non-pedigree        3-4
## 13 Cat072      7.23            11.38     2   Male 128 Non-pedigree          6
## 14 Cat076      7.77            12.43     2 Female 130 Non-pedigree        7-9
## 15 Cat078      7.90            12.02     2 Female 141 Non-pedigree        7-9
## 16 Cat081     10.37            10.86     2 Female 155 Non-pedigree        7-9
## 17 Cat090      7.84            12.23     2   Male 134     Pedigree          6
## 18 Cat092      9.00            12.45     2   Male 140     Pedigree          5
## 19 Cat094      9.05            13.02     2   Male 130 Non-pedigree        3-4
## 20 Cat095      7.98            11.96     2   Male 120 Non-pedigree        3-4
## 21 Cat103      9.92            13.59     2   Male 138     Pedigree          6
## 22 Cat105      7.44            11.12     2 Female 117 Non-pedigree        7-9
## 23 Cat110      7.04             9.60     2 Female 138 Non-pedigree          5
## 24 Cat112      8.64            12.61     2   Male 123 Non-pedigree          6
## 25 Cat122      8.00            11.55     2   Male 138 Non-pedigree        7-9
## 26 Cat140      7.03             9.91     2 Female 132 Non-pedigree        7-9
## 27 Cat141      9.03            11.90     2   Male 127 Non-pedigree        7-9
## 28 Cat152      7.04             9.01     2   Male 132 Non-pedigree        7-9
## 29 Cat167     10.31            13.05     2 Female 139 Non-pedigree        7-9
## 30 Cat190      9.01             9.52     2   Male 140 Non-pedigree        7-9
## 31 Cat197      7.05             9.07     2   Male 140 Non-pedigree          6
## 32 Cat198      8.05             8.52     2   Male 138 Non-pedigree          6
## 33 Cat203      7.04             9.23     2   Male 140 Non-pedigree          5
## 34 Cat204      7.02            10.64     2 Female 126 Non-pedigree        7-9

##d. examine healthy visits only

#exmaine healthy visits only?

#merge healthy_visit
Metadata_hyp_baseline_3 <- merge(
  Metadata_hyp_baseline2,
  Metadata_SBP2[, c("Cat_ID_timepoint", "Healthy_visit")],
  by = "Cat_ID_timepoint",
  all = FALSE
)
#make healthy the reference
Metadata_hyp_baseline_3  <- Metadata_hyp_baseline_3 %>%
  mutate(Healthy_visit= factor(
   Healthy_visit,
    levels = c("Yes", "No" )
  ))

colnames(Metadata_hyp_baseline_3)
##  [1] "Cat_ID_timepoint"                   "Cat_ID"                            
##  [3] "Owner_ID"                           "date"                              
##  [5] "dob"                                "Age_years"                         
##  [7] "Breed"                              "Sex"                               
##  [9] "BCS"                                "MCS_combined"                      
## [11] "hypertensive_sbp"                   "Hypertension"                      
## [13] "hypertensive_over.study"            "stHyp"                             
## [15] "X1st_visit_date"                    "X1st_visit_age"                    
## [17] "Censor_visit_date"                  "Censor_visit_age"                  
## [19] "baseline_dx"                        "BCS_group3"                        
## [21] "Heart_murmur"                       "HR"                                
## [23] "CKD"                                "Hyperthyroidism"                   
## [25] "Dental_disease"                     "oe.abnormalities_Yes_no_incomplete"
## [27] "MCS_binary"                         "SBP"                               
## [29] "Healthy_visit"

##e. inital Cox model exploring adding morbidities

cox_1<-coxph(Surv(Censor_visit_age, stHyp ) ~ Sex + Breed + SBP + BCS_group3 + Heart_murmur + HR + CKD + Hyperthyroidism, data =  Metadata_hyp_baseline2)
## Warning in coxph.fit(X, Y, istrat, offset, init, control, weights = weights, :
## Loglik converged before variable 10 ; coefficient may be infinite.
tbl_regression(cox_1, exp=T)%>% add_n(location="level")
Characteristic N HR 95% CI p-value
Sex



    Female 102 — —
    Male 88 2.58 1.16, 5.78 0.021
Breed



    Non-pedigree 163 — —
    Pedigree 27 1.21 0.37, 3.96 0.7
SBP 190 1.03 1.01, 1.06 0.007
BCS_group3



    5 54 — —
    3-4 29 1.78 0.52, 6.14 0.4
    6 47 0.99 0.28, 3.57 >0.9
    7-9 60 3.05 1.17, 7.98 0.023
Heart_murmur



    No 135 — —
    Yes 55 0.53 0.21, 1.29 0.2
HR 190 1.00 0.98, 1.02 0.8
CKD



    No 180 — —
    Yes 10 1.22 0.23, 6.51 0.8
Hyperthyroidism



    No 186 — —
    Yes 4 0.00 0.00, Inf >0.9
Abbreviations: CI = Confidence Interval, HR = Hazard Ratio 
# unable to model with hyperthyroidism - unstable coefficient. Remove? CKD also limitis event numbers

cox_2<-coxph(Surv(Censor_visit_age, stHyp ) ~ Sex + Breed + BCS_group3 + MCS_binary + Heart_murmur +CKD +Dental_disease + oe.abnormalities_Yes_no_incomplete, data = Metadata_hyp_baseline2)

tbl_regression(cox_2, exp=T)%>% add_n(location="level")
Characteristic N HR 95% CI p-value
Sex



    Female 97 — —
    Male 86 2.65 1.18, 5.96 0.018
Breed



    Non-pedigree 156 — —
    Pedigree 27 1.70 0.52, 5.54 0.4
BCS_group3



    5 52 — —
    3-4 28 2.14 0.63, 7.26 0.2
    6 47 0.78 0.21, 2.81 0.7
    7-9 56 3.05 1.11, 8.36 0.030
MCS_binary



    No 128 — —
    Yes 55 1.72 0.69, 4.28 0.2
Heart_murmur



    No 127 — —
    Yes 56 0.46 0.17, 1.23 0.12
CKD



    No 173 — —
    Yes 10 3.33 0.52, 21.2 0.2
Dental_disease



    No 71 — —
    Yes 112 0.63 0.28, 1.38 0.2
oe.abnormalities_Yes_no_incomplete



    No 48 — —
    Incomplete_OE 11 19.4 2.87, 131 0.002
    Yes 124 3.98 1.09, 14.5 0.037
Abbreviations: CI = Confidence Interval, HR = Hazard Ratio 
#strip down to healthy visit rather tahn co-morbs

cox_3<-coxph(Surv(Censor_visit_age, stHyp ) ~ Sex + Breed + BCS_group3 + MCS_binary + Healthy_visit, data = Metadata_hyp_baseline_3)

tbl_regression(cox_3, exp=T)%>% add_n(location="level")
Characteristic N HR 95% CI p-value
Sex



    Female 99 — —
    Male 86 2.53 1.14, 5.63 0.023
Breed



    Non-pedigree 158 — —
    Pedigree 27 1.69 0.58, 4.89 0.3
BCS_group3



    5 52 — —
    3-4 28 1.73 0.51, 5.83 0.4
    6 48 1.17 0.38, 3.59 0.8
    7-9 57 3.55 1.27, 9.96 0.016
MCS_binary



    No 130 — —
    Yes 55 1.92 0.81, 4.55 0.14
Healthy_visit



    Yes 134 — —
    No 51 0.55 0.20, 1.51 0.2
Abbreviations: CI = Confidence Interval, HR = Hazard Ratio 
cox.zph(cox_3)
##                 chisq df     p
## Sex            0.0343  1 0.853
## Breed          0.7912  1 0.374
## BCS_group3     3.5492  3 0.314
## MCS_binary     5.6851  1 0.017
## Healthy_visit  1.0205  1 0.312
## GLOBAL        11.9125  7 0.103
##issues - low event numbers of certain morbidities, healthy overall creates bias as unhealthy cats likely die earlier and are censored

##f. take healthy visit at baseline data only

cox_healthy_data<-Metadata_hyp_baseline_3%>%
  filter(Healthy_visit=="Yes")
cox_healthy<-coxph(Surv(Censor_visit_age, stHyp ) ~ Sex + Breed + BCS_group3 + MCS_binary, data = cox_healthy_data)

tbl_regression(cox_healthy, exp=T)%>% add_n(location="level")
Characteristic N HR 95% CI p-value
Sex



    Female 73 — —
    Male 61 2.68 1.12, 6.44 0.027
Breed



    Non-pedigree 115 — —
    Pedigree 19 1.77 0.55, 5.71 0.3
BCS_group3



    5 42 — —
    3-4 19 1.87 0.55, 6.34 0.3
    6 33 0.91 0.27, 3.07 0.9
    7-9 40 3.37 1.17, 9.71 0.024
MCS_binary



    No 102 — —
    Yes 32 1.54 0.57, 4.18 0.4
Abbreviations: CI = Confidence Interval, HR = Hazard Ratio 
#low sample size!

##g. forget about morbidities. Model using clinical data

#all
cox_hyprisk<-coxph(Surv(Censor_visit_age, stHyp ) ~ Sex + Breed + SBP + 
                     BCS_group3,
                   data = Metadata_hyp_baseline_for_cox)

tbl_regression(cox_hyprisk, exp=T)%>%add_n(location=c("level"))
Characteristic N HR 95% CI p-value
Sex



    Female 105 — —
    Male 91 2.43 1.15, 5.14 0.020
Breed



    Non-pedigree 169 — —
    Pedigree 27 1.31 0.44, 3.86 0.6
SBP 196 1.04 1.01, 1.06 0.003
BCS_group3



    5 54 — —
    3-4 29 1.73 0.51, 5.91 0.4
    6 50 0.96 0.30, 3.04 >0.9
    7-9 63 3.11 1.21, 8.03 0.019
Abbreviations: CI = Confidence Interval, HR = Hazard Ratio 
AIC(cox_hyprisk)
## [1] 275
#remove breed
cox_hyprisk2<-coxph(Surv(Censor_visit_age, stHyp ) ~ Sex + SBP + 
                     BCS_group3,
                   data = Metadata_hyp_baseline_for_cox)

tbl_regression(cox_hyprisk2, exp=T)%>%add_n(location=c("level"))
Characteristic N HR 95% CI p-value
Sex



    Female 105 — —
    Male 91 2.50 1.19, 5.24 0.016
SBP 196 1.04 1.01, 1.06 0.002
BCS_group3



    5 54 — —
    3-4 29 1.67 0.49, 5.67 0.4
    6 50 1.02 0.33, 3.13 >0.9
    7-9 63 3.05 1.19, 7.84 0.020
Abbreviations: CI = Confidence Interval, HR = Hazard Ratio 
AIC(cox_hyprisk2)
## [1] 273
#SBP alone
cox_hyprisk3<-coxph(Surv(Censor_visit_age, stHyp ) ~ SBP,
                   data = Metadata_hyp_baseline_for_cox)

tbl_regression(cox_hyprisk3, exp=T)%>%add_n(location=c("level"))
Characteristic N HR 95% CI p-value
SBP 196 1.03 1.01, 1.06 0.003
Abbreviations: CI = Confidence Interval, HR = Hazard Ratio 
AIC(cox_hyprisk3)
## [1] 278
AIC(cox_hyprisk, cox_hyprisk2, cox_hyprisk3)#cox_2 best fit
##              df AIC
## cox_hyprisk   6 275
## cox_hyprisk2  5 273
## cox_hyprisk3  1 278
summary(cox_hyprisk2)
## Call:
## coxph(formula = Surv(Censor_visit_age, stHyp) ~ Sex + SBP + BCS_group3, 
##     data = Metadata_hyp_baseline_for_cox)
## 
##   n= 196, number of events= 34 
## 
##                 coef exp(coef) se(coef)    z Pr(>|z|)   
## SexMale       0.9147    2.4961   0.3781 2.42   0.0155 * 
## SBP           0.0352    1.0358   0.0114 3.08   0.0021 **
## BCS_group33-4 0.5137    1.6714   0.6236 0.82   0.4101   
## BCS_group36   0.0218    1.0221   0.5714 0.04   0.9695   
## BCS_group37-9 1.1155    3.0510   0.4814 2.32   0.0205 * 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
##               exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
## SexMale            2.50      0.401     1.190      5.24
## SBP                1.04      0.965     1.013      1.06
## BCS_group33-4      1.67      0.598     0.492      5.67
## BCS_group36        1.02      0.978     0.333      3.13
## BCS_group37-9      3.05      0.328     1.188      7.84
## 
## Concordance= 0.73  (se = 0.043 )
## Likelihood ratio test= 21.4  on 5 df,   p=7e-04
## Wald test            = 19.8  on 5 df,   p=0.001
## Score (logrank) test = 21  on 5 df,   p=8e-04

###i. BEST MODEL

summary(cox_hyprisk2) 
## Call:
## coxph(formula = Surv(Censor_visit_age, stHyp) ~ Sex + SBP + BCS_group3, 
##     data = Metadata_hyp_baseline_for_cox)
## 
##   n= 196, number of events= 34 
## 
##                 coef exp(coef) se(coef)    z Pr(>|z|)   
## SexMale       0.9147    2.4961   0.3781 2.42   0.0155 * 
## SBP           0.0352    1.0358   0.0114 3.08   0.0021 **
## BCS_group33-4 0.5137    1.6714   0.6236 0.82   0.4101   
## BCS_group36   0.0218    1.0221   0.5714 0.04   0.9695   
## BCS_group37-9 1.1155    3.0510   0.4814 2.32   0.0205 * 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
##               exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
## SexMale            2.50      0.401     1.190      5.24
## SBP                1.04      0.965     1.013      1.06
## BCS_group33-4      1.67      0.598     0.492      5.67
## BCS_group36        1.02      0.978     0.333      3.13
## BCS_group37-9      3.05      0.328     1.188      7.84
## 
## Concordance= 0.73  (se = 0.043 )
## Likelihood ratio test= 21.4  on 5 df,   p=7e-04
## Wald test            = 19.8  on 5 df,   p=0.001
## Score (logrank) test = 21  on 5 df,   p=8e-04
tbl_regression(cox_hyprisk2, pvalue_fun= label_style_pvalue(digits = 3), exp=T)%>%add_n(location=c("level"))
Characteristic N HR 95% CI p-value
Sex



    Female 105 — —
    Male 91 2.50 1.19, 5.24 0.016
SBP 196 1.04 1.01, 1.06 0.002
BCS_group3



    5 54 — —
    3-4 29 1.67 0.49, 5.67 0.410
    6 50 1.02 0.33, 3.13 0.970
    7-9 63 3.05 1.19, 7.84 0.020
Abbreviations: CI = Confidence Interval, HR = Hazard Ratio

###ii. check PH assumptions

cox.zph(cox_hyprisk2)
##             chisq df    p
## Sex        0.0198  1 0.89
## SBP        0.0809  1 0.78
## BCS_group3 3.9355  3 0.27
## GLOBAL     4.0341  5 0.54
plot(cox.zph(cox_hyprisk2))

###iii. check outliers

# Standardized dfbeta residuals
residuals(cox_hyprisk2, type = "dfbeta")
##          [,1]      [,2]      [,3]      [,4]      [,5]
## 1   -4.07e-03  4.06e-04  2.97e-04 -4.85e-04 -5.23e-03
## 2   -1.13e-01  8.33e-04 -1.36e-03  1.06e-02 -1.16e-01

## 185  1.60e-02  1.39e-04 -1.98e-02  1.35e-01 -1.14e-02
## 186  1.63e-02 -1.47e-04 -1.75e-02  1.39e-01 -1.15e-02
## 187  1.15e-02  3.04e-04 -2.19e-02 -4.36e-03  1.83e-04

## 189  6.20e-05  3.05e-06  7.97e-05  6.97e-05  1.09e-04
## 190  0.00e+00  0.00e+00  0.00e+00  0.00e+00  0.00e+00
## 191  5.36e-02  8.38e-04 -1.98e-01 -1.81e-01 -1.73e-01
## 192 -8.03e-02 -8.74e-04  2.22e-02  1.91e-02  4.81e-02
## 193  6.80e-03  2.27e-04 -2.18e-03 -2.56e-03 -4.06e-03
## 194  4.06e-04  7.63e-06 -7.30e-05 -1.33e-04 -2.23e-04
## 195  0.00e+00  0.00e+00  0.00e+00  0.00e+00  0.00e+00
## 196  1.05e-02  3.14e-04 -4.24e-03 -1.60e-02  1.76e-04
plot(residuals(cox_hyprisk2, type = "dfbeta"))  # look for points far from 0

# Deviance residuals
plot(residuals(cox_hyprisk2, type = "deviance"))

###iv. check linearity of SBP

# Fit model with a penalized spline for SBP
cox_spline <- coxph(Surv(Censor_visit_age, stHyp) ~ ns(SBP, df=2) + Sex + BCS_group3,
                    data = Metadata_hyp_baseline_for_cox)
summary(cox_spline)  # or compare log-likelihood to linear model
## Call:
## coxph(formula = Surv(Censor_visit_age, stHyp) ~ ns(SBP, df = 2) + 
##     Sex + BCS_group3, data = Metadata_hyp_baseline_for_cox)
## 
##   n= 196, number of events= 34 
## 
##                       coef exp(coef)  se(coef)     z Pr(>|z|)   
## ns(SBP, df = 2)1  9.28e+00  1.08e+04  3.01e+00  3.08   0.0021 **
## ns(SBP, df = 2)2  9.80e-01  2.66e+00  1.34e+00  0.73   0.4650   
## SexMale           8.34e-01  2.30e+00  3.78e-01  2.21   0.0271 * 
## BCS_group33-4     7.43e-01  2.10e+00  6.30e-01  1.18   0.2381   
## BCS_group36      -8.73e-02  9.16e-01  5.72e-01 -0.15   0.8787   
## BCS_group37-9     1.12e+00  3.06e+00  4.82e-01  2.32   0.0204 * 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
##                  exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
## ns(SBP, df = 2)1  1.08e+04   9.29e-05    29.223  3.96e+06
## ns(SBP, df = 2)2  2.66e+00   3.75e-01     0.192  3.69e+01
## SexMale           2.30e+00   4.34e-01     1.099  4.83e+00
## BCS_group33-4     2.10e+00   4.76e-01     0.612  7.23e+00
## BCS_group36       9.16e-01   1.09e+00     0.299  2.81e+00
## BCS_group37-9     3.06e+00   3.27e-01     1.189  7.87e+00
## 
## Concordance= 0.768  (se = 0.038 )
## Likelihood ratio test= 27.5  on 6 df,   p=1e-04
## Wald test            = 21.7  on 6 df,   p=0.001
## Score (logrank) test = 22.4  on 6 df,   p=0.001
AIC(cox_hyprisk2, cox_spline)
##              df AIC
## cox_hyprisk2  5 273
## cox_spline    6 269
#some slight imp to model fit but ideally keep the simpler linear model for easier interpretation

###v. assess concordance and c-index

#Concordance = 0.730
concordance(cox_hyprisk2)$concordance
## [1] 0.73
summary(cox_hyprisk2)
## Call:
## coxph(formula = Surv(Censor_visit_age, stHyp) ~ Sex + SBP + BCS_group3, 
##     data = Metadata_hyp_baseline_for_cox)
## 
##   n= 196, number of events= 34 
## 
##                 coef exp(coef) se(coef)    z Pr(>|z|)   
## SexMale       0.9147    2.4961   0.3781 2.42   0.0155 * 
## SBP           0.0352    1.0358   0.0114 3.08   0.0021 **
## BCS_group33-4 0.5137    1.6714   0.6236 0.82   0.4101   
## BCS_group36   0.0218    1.0221   0.5714 0.04   0.9695   
## BCS_group37-9 1.1155    3.0510   0.4814 2.32   0.0205 * 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
##               exp(coef) exp(-coef) lower .95 upper .95
## SexMale            2.50      0.401     1.190      5.24
## SBP                1.04      0.965     1.013      1.06
## BCS_group33-4      1.67      0.598     0.492      5.67
## BCS_group36        1.02      0.978     0.333      3.13
## BCS_group37-9      3.05      0.328     1.188      7.84
## 
## Concordance= 0.73  (se = 0.043 )
## Likelihood ratio test= 21.4  on 5 df,   p=7e-04
## Wald test            = 19.8  on 5 df,   p=0.001
## Score (logrank) test = 21  on 5 df,   p=8e-04

###vi. check for multicollinearity

vif(cox_hyprisk2)
##       SexMale           SBP BCS_group33-4   BCS_group36 BCS_group37-9 
##          1.10          1.03          1.62          1.79          1.92
#all VIF<2

vii. bootstrap

# Function to compute coefficients in bootstrap sample
cox_boot_fun <- function(data, indices) {
  d <- data[indices, ]  # resample with replacement
  fit <- coxph(Surv(Censor_visit_age, stHyp) ~ Sex + SBP + BCS_group3, data = d)
  return(coef(fit))  # return coefficients
}

# Run bootstrap
set.seed(123)
boot_results <- boot(data = Metadata_hyp_baseline_for_cox,
                     statistic = cox_boot_fun,
                     R = 1000)  # number of bootstrap samples
## Warning in coxph.fit(X, Y, istrat, offset, init, control, weights = weights, :
## Loglik converged before variable 3,4,5 ; coefficient may be infinite.
## Warning in coxph.fit(X, Y, istrat, offset, init, control, weights = weights, :
## Loglik converged before variable 3 ; coefficient may be infinite.
## Warning in coxph.fit(X, Y, istrat, offset, init, control, weights = weights, :
## Loglik converged before variable 3,4,5 ; coefficient may be infinite.
## Warning in coxph.fit(X, Y, istrat, offset, init, control, weights = weights, :
## Loglik converged before variable 3 ; coefficient may be infinite.
## Warning in coxph.fit(X, Y, istrat, offset, init, control, weights = weights, :
## Loglik converged before variable 3 ; coefficient may be infinite.
## Warning in coxph.fit(X, Y, istrat, offset, init, control, weights = weights, :
## Loglik converged before variable 3,4,5 ; coefficient may be infinite.
# Inspect results
boot_results
## 
## ORDINARY NONPARAMETRIC BOOTSTRAP
## 
## 
## Call:
## boot(data = Metadata_hyp_baseline_for_cox, statistic = cox_boot_fun, 
##     R = 1000)
## 
## 
## Bootstrap Statistics :
##     original   bias    std. error
## t1*   0.9147  0.07072      0.4281
## t2*   0.0352  0.00342      0.0126
## t3*   0.5137 -0.04494      1.5899
## t4*   0.0218  0.04590      1.2318
## t5*   1.1155  0.11581      1.1957
# Bootstrap mean, SD, and 95% CI
apply(boot_results$t, 2, mean)      # mean coefficients
## [1] 0.9854 0.0386 0.4687 0.0677 1.2313
apply(boot_results$t, 2, sd)        # SD of coefficients
## [1] 0.4281 0.0126 1.5899 1.2318 1.1957
# 95% percentile CI
apply(boot_results$t, 2, function(x) quantile(x, probs = c(0.025, 0.975)))
##        [,1]   [,2]   [,3]  [,4]  [,5]
## 2.5%  0.244 0.0168 -0.815 -1.25 0.209
## 97.5% 1.968 0.0656  1.902  1.38 2.456
exp(apply(boot_results$t, 2, mean))  # mean HRs
## [1] 2.68 1.04 1.60 1.07 3.43
exp(apply(boot_results$t, 2, function(x) quantile(x, probs=c(0.025,0.975))))  # 95% CI
##       [,1] [,2]  [,3]  [,4]  [,5]
## 2.5%  1.28 1.02 0.443 0.287  1.23
## 97.5% 7.16 1.07 6.702 3.972 11.66

run rms

# Setup for rms functions
dd <- datadist(Metadata_hyp_baseline_for_cox)
options(datadist="dd")

cox_rms <- cph(Surv(Censor_visit_age, stHyp) ~ Sex + SBP + BCS_group3,
               data = Metadata_hyp_baseline_for_cox, x=TRUE, y=TRUE, surv=TRUE)

# Bootstrap validation
set.seed(123)
validate(cox_rms, B=1000, method="boot")  # B=1000 bootstrap samples
##       index.orig training   test optimism index.corrected   Lower Upper    n
## Dxy       0.4604   0.5042 0.4354   0.0688          0.3916  0.2074 0.571 1000
## R2        0.1348   0.1642 0.1123   0.0520          0.0828 -0.0440 0.181 1000
## Slope     1.0000   1.0000 0.8060   0.1940          0.8060  0.3269 1.354 1000
## D         0.0715   0.0904 0.0585   0.0319          0.0396 -0.0409 0.101 1000
## U        -0.0070  -0.0073 0.0117  -0.0190          0.0120 -0.0110 0.124 1000
## Q         0.0786   0.0976 0.0468   0.0509          0.0277 -0.1398 0.107 1000
## g         1.0430   1.2282 0.9330   0.2952          0.7478 -0.3047 1.266 1000

##vii. plot km curve

#KM
km1<-survfit2(Surv(Censor_visit_age, stHyp ) ~ Sex, data = Metadata_hyp_baseline_for_cox)%>%
  ggsurvfit() +
  labs(x = "Age (years)", y="Hypertension-free survival probability")+
  ggtitle("A. Hypertension-free survival by sex")+
  #add_risktable()+
  add_confidence_interval()+
  add_censor_mark( size=1)+
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 6:17, limits = c(6, 17))+
theme(
  legend.position = "inside",
  legend.position.inside = c(0.15, 0.15),
  legend.text=element_text(size=9),
  legend.box.background =  element_rect(colour="black", linewidth = 1),
  legend.title = element_text(size=9), 
  legend.title.position="top"
)
km1
## Warning: Removed 2 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_step()`).

#ggsave("Hypertension_sex_KM_curev170326.png", #plot=last_plot(),height = 2100, width = 2100, #units="px" )


#KM
km2<-survfit2(Surv(Censor_visit_age, stHyp ) ~ BCS_group3, data = Metadata_hyp_baseline_for_cox)%>%
  ggsurvfit() +
  labs(x = "Age (years)", y="Hypertension-free survival probability", color="BCS")+
  ggtitle("B. Hypertension-free survival by BCS")+
 # add_risktable()+
  #add_confidence_interval()+
  scale_color_manual(values=c("3-4" = "black", "5" = "brown", "6" = "aquamarine4", "7-9" = "grey"))+ 
  add_censor_mark( size=1)+
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 6:17, limits = c(6, 17))+
  theme(
  legend.position = "inside",
  legend.position.inside = c(0.1, 0.2),
  legend.text=element_text(size=9),
  legend.box.background =  element_rect(colour="black", linewidth = 1),
  legend.title = element_text(size=9)
)
km2
## Warning: Removed 4 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_step()`).

#ggsave("Hypertension_BCS_KM_curev170326.png", #plot=last_plot(),height = 2100, width = 2100, #units="px" )

km1+km2
## Warning: Removed 2 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_step()`).
## Warning: Removed 4 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_step()`).

#ggsave("Hypertension_sex_bcs_KM_curve_Figure170326.png"#, plot=last_plot(),height = 15, width = 30, units="cm" #)
